programme.desc: Return to a world of two realities: one, everyday life; the other, what lies behind it. To find out if his reality is a construct, to truly know himself, Mr. Anderson will have to choose to follow the white rabbit once more.

programme.title: The Polar Express
programme.desc: This is the story of a young hero boy who boards on a powerful magical train that's headed to the North Pole and Santa Claus' home on Christmas Eve night. During this ride, he embarks on a journey of self-discovery which shows him that the wonder of life never fades for those who believe.

programme.title: Final Destination 3
programme.desc: Wendy Christensen and a group of teens who escaped a fatal roller-coaster crash face a bloody date with Death.

programme.title: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: A wealthy entrepreneur secretly creates a theme park featuring living dinosaurs drawn from prehistoric DNA. Before opening day, he invites a team of experts and his two eager grandchildren to experience the park and help calm anxious investors. However, the park is anything but amusing as the security systems go off-line and the dinosaurs escape.

programme.title: Cinema Paradiso
programme.desc: Salvatore, a famous film director, returns to his hometown for the funeral of the local theater's film projectionist, Alfredo. He reminisces about his life as a young boy falling in love with cinema.

programme.title: Bob Marley: One Love
programme.desc: The story of how reggae icon Bob Marley overcame adversity, and the journey behind his revolutionary music.

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: Chronicles the experiences of a formerly successful banker as a prisoner in the gloomy jailhouse of Shawshank after being found guilty of a crime he did not commit. The film portrays the man's unique way of dealing with his new, torturous life; along the way he befriends a number of fellow prisoners, most notably a wise long-term inmate named Red.

programme.title: The Boy, the Mole, the Fox and the Horse
programme.desc: Charlie Mackesy's bestselling book comes to life in this beautifully animated film from NoneMore and Bad Robot Productions. Follow the unlikely bond between four friends as they explore the meaning of kindness, friendship, courage and hope. A story for viewers of all ages in a heartwarming, classically animated film.

programme.title: Spirited Away
programme.desc: The fanciful adventures of a ten-year-old girl named Chihiro, who discovers a secret world when she and her family get lost and venture through a hillside tunnel. When her parents undergo a mysterious transformation, Chihiro must fend for herself as she encounters strange spirits, assorted creatures and a grumpy sorceress who seeks to prevent her from returning to the human world.

programme.title: Kiki's Delivery Service
programme.desc: Along with her black cat Jiji, Kiki settles in a seaside town and starts a high-flying delivery service. Here begins her magical encounter with independence and responsibility, making lifelong friends and finding her place in the world.

programme.title: A Minecraft Movie
programme.desc: Four misfits are suddenly pulled through a mysterious portal into a bizarre cubic wonderland that thrives on imagination. To get back home they'll have to master this world while embarking on a quest with an unexpected expert crafter.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
programme.desc: John Wick uncovers a path to defeating The High Table. But before he can earn his freedom, Wick must face off against a new enemy with powerful alliances across the globe and forces that turn old friends into foes.

programme.title: The Naked Gun
programme.desc: Only one man has the particular set of skills... to lead Police Squad and save the world: Lt. Frank Drebin Jr. Following in his father's footsteps, he must solve a murder case to prevent Police Squad from closure.

programme.title: Shaun of the Dead
programme.desc: Shaun (Simon Pegg) doesn't have a very good day, so he decides to turn his life around by getting his ex to take him back, but he times it for right in the middle of what may be a zombie apocalypse. But for him, it's an opportunity to show everyone he knows how useful he is by saving them all. All he has to do is survive, and get his ex back.

programme.title: Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo
programme.desc: 14 years have passed since the near third impact. Most of the world has changed except Shinji Ikari who awakens, unaged in a new and strange environment. Misato has formed a group that is separate from Nerv. The fight is far from over but the biggest struggle might be against humans and former allies.

programme.title: Ratatouille
programme.desc: A rat who can cook makes an unusual alliance with a young kitchen worker at a famous Paris restaurant.

programme.title: Alice in Wonderland
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old Alice returns to the magical world from her childhood adventure, where she reunites with her old friends and learns of her true destiny: to end the Red Queen's reign of terror.

programme.title: Five Nights at Freddy's
programme.desc: A troubled security guard begins working at Freddy Fazbear's Pizza. During his five nights on the job, he realizes that something is wrong with the pizzeria and pretty soon finds the truth about its animatronics.

programme.title: The Cat Returns
programme.desc: After helping a cat, a seventeen-year-old girl finds herself involuntarily engaged to a cat Prince in a magical world where her only hope of freedom lies with a dapper cat statuette come to life.

programme.title: The Shining
programme.desc: A family heads to an isolated hotel for the winter, where a sinister presence influences the father into violence. At the same time, his psychic son sees horrifying forebodings from both the past and the future.

programme.title: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
programme.desc: Two Jedi escape a hostile blockade to find allies and come across a young boy who may bring balance to the Force, but the long-dormant Sith resurface to claim their former glory.

programme.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
programme.desc: While the Civil War rages on between the Union and the Confederacy, three men – a quiet loner, a ruthless hitman, and a Mexican bandit – comb the American Southwest in search of a strongbox containing $200,000 in stolen gold.

programme.title: Cars 2
programme.desc: Star race car Lightning McQueen and his pal Mater head overseas to compete in the World Grand Prix race. But the road to the championship becomes rocky as Mater gets caught up in an intriguing adventure of his own: international espionage.

programme.title: Iron Man 2
programme.desc: With the world now aware of his dual life as the armored superhero Iron Man, billionaire inventor Tony Stark faces pressure from the government, the press, and the public to share his technology with the military. Unwilling to let go of his invention, Stark, along with Pepper Potts, and James "Rhodey" Rhodes at his side, must forge new alliances - and confront powerful enemies.

programme.title: Moana
programme.desc: In ancient Polynesia, when a terrible curse incurred by the demigod Maui reaches Moana's island, she answers the Ocean's call to seek out Maui to set things right.

programme.title: Deadpool
programme.desc: This is the origin story of former Special Forces operative turned mercenary Wade Wilson, who after being subjected to a rogue experiment that leaves him with accelerated healing powers, adopts the alter ego Deadpool. Armed with his new abilities and a dark, twisted sense of humor, Deadpool hunts down the man who nearly destroyed his life.

programme.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
programme.desc: When a mysterious artifact is uncovered on the Moon, a spacecraft manned by two humans and one supercomputer is sent to Jupiter to find its origins.

programme.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs
programme.desc: Inventor Flint Lockwood creates a machine that makes clouds rain food, enabling the down-and-out citizens of Chewandswallow to feed themselves. But when the falling food reaches gargantuan proportions, Flint must scramble to avert disaster. Can he regain control of the machine and put an end to the wild weather before the town is destroyed?

programme.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
programme.desc: Ever since US Diplomatic Security Service Agent Hobbs and lawless outcast Shaw first faced off, they just have traded smack talk and body blows. But when cyber-genetically enhanced anarchist Brixton's ruthless actions threaten the future of humanity, they join forces to defeat him.

programme.title: Ted 2
programme.desc: Newlywed couple Ted and Tami-Lynn want to have a baby, but in order to qualify to be a parent, Ted will have to prove he's a person in a court of law.

programme.title: Wonka
programme.desc: Willy Wonka – chock-full of ideas and determined to change the world one delectable bite at a time – is proof that the best things in life begin with a dream, and if you’re lucky enough to meet Willy Wonka, anything is possible.

programme.title: GoldenEye
programme.desc: When a powerful secret defense system is stolen, James Bond is assigned to stop a Russian crime syndicate from using it.

programme.title: Cars
programme.desc: While traveling to California for the dispute of the final race of the Piston Cup against The King and Chick Hicks, the famous Lightning McQueen accidentally damages the road of the small town Radiator Springs and is sentenced to repair it. Lightning McQueen has to work hard and finds friendship and love in the simple locals, changing its values during his stay in the small town and becoming a true winner.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The MythBusters are actually taking a pot shot at an ancient Chinese text that alludes to a subterranean sonar system that could apparently detect and intercept an invasion from below. Also, if food's on the floor less than five seconds is it safe to eat?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the final, and the remaining bakers are set three challenges that will test every aspect of their baking skills, including a classic carrot cake and a Mad Hatter's tea party banquet. One baker will be crowned the Bake Off 2021 winner.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Whether it's full blown road rage or just the ever-present frustration at life's little inconveniences, we all feel a little mad sometimes. But what if getting angry had less to do with what's going on outside, and more to do with what's going on inside your brain? In this episode we'll explore why your brain gets angry and what you can do about it. Through a series of games and experiments, we'll prove to you that anger isn't always a bad thing, and how having a short fuse might not be the worst trait to have. Also you'll learn how to deal better with the anger of other people, and your own.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 70-year-old George is rushed to A&E after he fell and impaled his eye on a piece of driftwood. Debby, who is 59, has cut her hand in her lawnmower while removing some grass cuttings. Phoebe, 8, has scissors stuck in her thigh.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
It's day two and the competition has moved to Moaps Farm in Dane Hill, East Sussex, where Lesley Miller hopes her guests will be charmed by her laid back approach.
On arrival the group are blown away by the view, but at breakfast, Lesley's casual approach leads to chaos.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The third visit of the week takes the B&B'ers to Jabajak in Carmarthenshire, Wales, where sisters Lorraine Lloyd and Amanda Stuart-Robson host in their vineyard and restaurant B&B.
The group are set the task of attempting to herd local Welsh sheep, and 'sheep whisperer' Lesley comes into her own.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Hospital Laundry, Brass Instrument Restoration, Horse Replicas, Excavation Buckets
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Hospital Laundry, Brass Instrument Restoration, Horse Replicas, and Excavation Buckets and shows how each is made.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.sub-title: You've Got a Friend in Me
programme.desc: 60-year-old bricklayer Phil is rushed to A&E in a critical condition after falling just two feet off a ladder at work and suffering multiple injuries. Violet, who is 93, has fallen and seriously fractured her leg.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The last visit of the competition is to the Clarendon Royal in Gravesend, Kent, where owner Les Woollends and his head chef Lesz Zajak want to dazzle their guests.
On arrival, the guests are suitably impressed, but Lesley Miller suspects the champagne truffles and fruit bowl are a ploy to win her over.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The American Civil War was fought with polished bayonets, unreliable muskets and bulky cannons. But was that all? Not according to the Mythbusters: Adam, Jamie and the build team join forces to find out if the Confederate Army had a secret deadly weapon.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Corey, who is 27, is rushed to St George's after suffering a cardiac arrest during a game of ice hockey. John, 55, fell 10 feet while taking down scaffolding at home.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Meet modern-day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard. With demanding customers, high turnover, and one of the biggest decorative salvage yards in the UK, what will he come across today?

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Experience life in the most hostile corners of the planet by exploring the terrain, seasons, and animals of the Arctic and Antarctic.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day, and the B&Bers meet for the last time to discuss their feedback forms and see how much they've been paid. With low scoring and hurt feelings, there's plenty of tension before the winner is revealed.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 17-year-old Shea has serious stab wounds and is sent to a high dependency unit. Bob, also 17, arrives after impaling his leg on a fence while taking a shortcut home from his school. 33-year-old James is brought in by ambulance after fainting at home.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Have you ever noticed that life is a series of routines, done in the same order, day after day? Whether you realize it or not, your daily routine is really a pattern. Learn how, when processing the world, your brain is always filling in the blanks.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Lewis, who is 18, has come off a motorbike and collided with two cars. Sam, 27, fainted and cut his lips while smoking a Cuban cigar at a barbecue.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off is back as 12 new bakers enter the iconic white tent. It’s cake week, and the bakers tackle a striking sponge and a scaled-down version of a home close to their hearts.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The first B&B of the week is Hazels Roost in Bainbridge, Yorkshire, where Lithuanian-raised host Millie Bussey hopes her years working on cruise ships will mean the competition is all plain sailing.
An afternoon of bowls gets the competitive juices flowing, and the next morning, one of the guest's young son attempts to entertain the guests with a magic show.

programme.title: Look Around You
programme.desc: A selection of mock Ceefax pages related to the first series.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Ceramic Fireplaces, Synthetic Corks, Parking Garage Floor Slabs
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Ceramic Fireplaces, Synthetic Corks, and Parking Garage Floor Slabs and shows how each is made.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Michelle, who is 49, is rushed to St George's after colliding with a car on a dual carriageway. Frankie, 19, has been thrown from her horse. 11-year-old Merlin has hurt his wrist.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: Gordon takes on a nightmare restaurant abroad - La Parra de Burriana in Nerja on the Spanish Costa del Sol. Nestling among the cafes lining the seafront offering all-day English breakfast and chipsEx-nightclub manager Laurence set himself up in business 18 months ago with a loan from his dad and, although he's not an experienced chef he mans the kitchen on his own determined to offer something better than chips.Laurence's menu has 72 dishes including his signature dish: prawns in garlic with chocolate sauce. Running front-of-house is Laurence's mate, Alex and sous-chef, Norman, is on the barbecue serving kebabs and steaks cooked by torchlight. The kebabs are raw, the dining room is a mess and anyone who does turn up for diner faces a interminable wait. Laurence lost £22k last year but still resists Gordon's attempts to introduce a simple menu that will bring customers back.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
At Rhandregynwen Hall in Llanymynech, even host Mark Johannsen and his friend Amos Bell have trouble with the B&B's name.
The competition revs up with some dirt buggy racing, and in the morning Mark feels the heat as blunders with the breakfast orders put him on the back foot.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The last of the air-cooled legends. Mike goes in search of what many fans consider to be the last truly great Porsche. What he finds is the cheapest, tattiest 993 in the country. Mike however, can see a mechanically sound motor and potential profit lurking beneath seventeen years of muck and grime. Edd sets to work on a rescue mission to restore the car’s racing pedigree. It’s unloved and underpowered but after Edd lavishes some care and attention on the car, will the smart engine fixes and custom handling package spell profit on a Porsche that is still major money?

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The team work together to test whether two trucks could crash head-on with enough force to fuse together and completely flatten a car between them, and test vodka as a treatment for foot odour and bad breath.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: David, who is 29, is airlifted to St George's following a head on collision at speed. An 18-year-old breaks his leg playing football. A great-grandmother falls down the stairs.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Secret preparations accelerate for the opening of Jeremy's restaurant.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Kim, who is 58, has been run over by a lorry and suffered severe blood loss. Patrick, 65, has fallen from a loft ladder.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The third visit is to the Smart and Simple hotel in Tunbridge Wells, Kent, where owner Simon Colbran and his assistant manager Rebecca Heasman want their rivals to agree 'the rooms are smart, and that's simple'.
Ex-footballer Simon takes his guests for a good kicking on the pitch, but ends up with egg on his face after an early pratfall. At dinner, Cathy's joke about Lithuanian-raised Millie's accent leaves her fuming.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob visit their most dazzling and extraordinary destination to date, the island of North Uist in the Outer Hebrides. Completely unlike anywhere they have fished before, this small, remote but beautiful island has an intricate network of tidal pools and sea lochs, making it near enough equal parts land and water and therefore the perfect place to fish for sea trout.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Lee, who is 36, is rushed to St George's after being involved in a high-speed road traffic collision. 34-year-old Jeroen has been knocked off his bike. Chris, who is 21 years old and a vlogger, has dislocated his finger playing basketball.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: The power of intuition--when you know something to be true without knowing why--is one of the mind's greatest mysteries. Test your intuition and learn how your brain makes decisions without you having to think about it.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Ace salvager Drew Pritchard is always on the lookout for stock for his show room. Today, it’s full steam ahead at Trinity Marine in Devon, but it’s not all plain sailing.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Adam and Jamie test the ability of steel toe boots to protect against heavy objects. In Bottle Blast Off, the Build team fills a soda bottle and watches as it lifts off, testing Newton's third law: every action has an equal and opposite reaction.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The final visit of the week is to Pearfield B&B in Petersfield, where owner Cathy Robinson and her son Alex want to win over their guests with a home from home experience.
Tennis club champion Cathy takes her guests for a knockabout, but while she wins over her guests with her patience, it's guest Simon who emerges victorious.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Oil Pressure Sensors, Large Format Printing, Heavy Equipment Simulators, Head & Neck Restraints
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Oil Pressure Sensors, Large Format Printing, Heavy Equipment Simulators, and Head & Neck Restraints and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day, and the B&Bers meet for the last time to discuss their feedback forms and see how much they've been paid. With low scoring and hurt feelings, there's plenty of tension before the winner is revealed.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It’s biscuit week, and the bakers tackle illusion macarons and a fruity favourite, before unleashing their creativity by making a dramatic 3D mask entirely from biscuits.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Joan, who is 80, has severe pain in her chest, arm and jaw. Roofer Mark, 54, has fallen 10 feet off a ladder. Josie fell and hit her head while performing a Dirty Dancing routine on a hen do.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit is to Edenmore Guest House in Ardrossan, Scotland, where host Hugh Begbie hopes his guests aren't the touchy-feely type.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Emma, who is 47, needs a CT scan following a cycling collision with a bus. 33-year-old Josh has severe back pain. Pamela, 87, is worried that she may have a blood clot in her leg.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mike, who is 79, collapsed in the street while walking his dog. Andrew, 53, tripped on a paving slab on his way to work and has a suspected dislocated shoulder.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: From electric shock therapy to homeopathic tongue tinglers, can the MythBusters find a cure to seasickness? Does driving with your pickup's tailgate down save gas and, hence, money?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
Iin Yorkshire, host Diane Lowe makes the most of her area's Calendar Girls connections and the battle is on to create the best Victoria sponge.
Next morning, the eggs Benedict are a huge hit with the guests and some glowing comments leave Diane close to tears.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A near-perfect ‘50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage ‘80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire, but traffic chaos on the way to Wales puts him way behind schedule. "I can't be late, I've got no pyjamas" ...says a worried Derek as he realises he is going to have to overnight across the border. Safely back in Yorkshire, he evaluates the cars ahead of the auction - two of the best he has seen. The vehicles' owners have high expectations, the reserves are top end - and the auction is days away. Paul picks up a stunning Matchless Model X motorbike which attracts a lot of pre-auction attention from some vintage motorcycle fans, and Derek and grandson Charlie spend the day at Stokesley Classic Car Show. It's not a relaxing day out for the pair as they've been set a monumental task - valuing the 200 classic cars.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Oxfordshire, hosts Andrew and Jeanette Collette's rural retreat is a bit too peaceful for some of their louder guests.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: From knowing when to cross the street to avoiding hot stoves, your brain is constantly making judgments that you likely take for granted. We chalk it up to common sense - but just how common is it? Through interactive games and mind-bending challenges, unravel your mental shortcuts and learn how to harness the power of collective thinking. Play along to understand how your common sense can either lead you astray or keep you on the right track.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 64-year-old John is brought to St George's in an induced coma, having been thrown from his bicycle at high speed. Florian, who is 21 and a drama student, tripped and hurt his foot rushing to class. Ron, 82, is rushed to A&E with a critically low heart rate of under 40 beats per minute.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Stuart, who is 40, is transferred from another hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision with multiple cyclists during a race. Elicia-Jean, 83, collapsed at home after experiencing dizziness from a suspected infection.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Mobile Concert Stages, Mascara, Continuous Miners, Wood Gift Boxes
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Mobile Concert Stages, Mascara, Continuous Miners, and Wood Gift Boxes and shows how each is made.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It’s Bread Week, and the bakers put their stamp on a classic pizza, get in a twist with a perfect pastry and tackle a showstopping Smörgåstårta, a decorative Danish sandwich cake.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee look for potentially lucrative leads at a Catholic girls' school in Worcestershire; a visit to a Welsh prop hire firm.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Adam and Jamie unleash a deadly myth from a supermax prison, attempting to make a deadly weapon out of newspaper and underwear. Grant, Kari and Tory unscrew the cap on some more vodka myths, such as removing cigarette smell from clothing.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An unknown patient arrives after collapsing and falling face first in the street. 29-year-old Rick comes to minors after an accident at the gym. Marigold, 3, has signs of a chest infection.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: Gordon travels to the seaside in aid of hotel and restaurant the Sandgate. This is happily married couple Lois and Peters' first venture into the catering industry. They used to enjoy eating in restaurants, so thought it might be fun to own one. A year and a half later their dream has turned into a nightmare - they're losing £4,000 a month. In the kitchen, their head chef Stuart is on the brink of a nervous breakdown. He's forced to cook for four restaurants, including fine dining upstairs and Japanese fare in the basement. But it's when he loses his coveted AA award that he really hits rock bottom. Eccentric, overstaffed and on the point of collapse, can Gordon bring this real life Fawlty Towers back from the edge?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The final visit of the week is to the George and Dragon, in Princes Risborough, where landlords Ian and Elle hope an afternoon of ghost-hunting will put their guests in high spirits.
At breakfast, the impressive portion sizes are overshadowed when the chef bares his bottom.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Emotions run high as the rivals confront each other face to face and discover what they've been paid, before the winner is announced.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: We return to Diddly Squat to find the farm in turmoil. The Council has shut down the restaurant, the weather is making planting crops impossible and costs are on the up. Jeremy needs to formulate a new plan to get his farm through the year.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish at the estate lake of Burghley House, near Stamford. Their target is the bream, but who knows what else they might catch in this rarely fished water.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Marie, who is 31, arrives in A&E by ambulance after collapsing at home with an unexplained headache. 10-month-old Erin has a build-up of wax in her ears following a cold and fever. Eileen, 87, comes in with shortness of breath and a high heart rate.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's BIG
programme.desc: Richard ventures 2km beneath the Alps into the world's longest underground railway connection, the Brenner Base Tunnel, riding some giant machines!

programme.title: Ramsay's Costa Del Nightmares
programme.desc: Gordon travels to the stunning whitewashed mountain village of Gaucin, half an hour from the Costa del Sol, and a brand new up-market restaurant that's already heading for disaster.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Whether you're a left-handed artist or a right-handed accountant, we all use both sides of our brains. But is there any science behind the notion that the left half or our brain is the creative side while the right half is more logical? In this episode about your left and right brain, we're going to put you through a series of games and experiments designed to find out how the two sides operate, what they really control, and whether or not one side of your brain dominates the other.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This shocking episode follows three middle-aged women who are stabbed in broad daylight in a random attack at a supermarket car park in Surrey. Jean, who's 67, had gone to Sainsbury's to get macaroni cheese for her grandson and was stabbed in the back while carrying shopping to her car. Doctors are worried that the wound may have hit a vital organ or a major vessel and she's sent for urgent CT scans. Meanwhile, her daughter Clair recalls growing up in a pub in Fulham where her mum was the landlady: 'I remember a couple of guys came in with machetes yelling and shouting and she gave them a jolly good talking to... she's always been feisty... she can throw someone on the floor if she needs to'. Rani, who's 71, was stabbed twice in the back while waiting for a bus.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week kicks off at Langsmeade House in Thame, Oxfordshire, with Dutch-born owner Marianne Aben and her trusty housekeeper Fran Birchwood. Their quirky hosting style and 1920s knick knacks are a hit with Michael Prime and his mum Lorna, even if the dusty bedframe isn't.

programme.title: Mind Field
programme.desc: We may value having Freedom of Choice, but are we actually happier when we have limited choices...or even no choice at all? Do we truly have control over our decisions, or are they really predetermined by other forces? My fellow YouTubers and I have our minds read by a “box” that reveals who - or what - is really calling the shots.

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Deserts are lands of such brutal extremes they have shaped remarkable survival stories.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: With its legendary mix of unique technology and awesome performance, Mazda’s coupe is unlike any other. Mike finds one that appears to have nothing wrong with it and challenges Edd to make a good car even better. But these cars are complex and unforgiving. So while Edd handles with care, Mike sorts out some stylish mods to make the RX7’s looks match its performance.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Is the photo of a light plane, with precise, clean tears on the side, genuine? Was it the work of the jilted lover, who took to her ex's plane with a chainsaw and a taste for vengeance?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Natasha and Dai Pollock juggle the demands of bringing up a young family with running their B&B in Stratford-upon-Avon. On arrival, entrepreneur Mark Genders finds some 'snagging' issues in the décor and Michael Prime and his mum Lorna are 'mortified' when they discover a short and curly in the drawers.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Gary, who is 50, is brought in by ambulance after a garage collapsed and fell on his legs. Maggie, 83, comes in to A&E with suspected sepsis. Baby George comes in with his mum after drinking nail varnish remover.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: NASCAR Car Bodies, Hurley Sticks, Tube Power Amplifiers, Thermal Coffee Pots
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for NASCAR Car Bodies, Hurley Sticks, Tube Power Amplifiers, and Thermal Coffee Pots and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to pet-friendly Edmund House in Deal in Kent where owner Michael Prime is joined by his doting mum Lorna. On arrival, Marianne Abben and Fran Birchwood love the seaside-inspired room, but entrepreneur Mark Genders is far from thrilled by the 'no frills' experience.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Adam, 20, is brought in by ambulance after being involved in a high-speed motorcycle accident. 70-year-old Richard comes to A&E with a high heart rate. Pia, who is 10, has fallen and hurt her knee.

programme.title: Look Around You
programme.desc: We visit the Look Around You Music 2000 Competition, a search for the sounds people will be listening to in the year 2000. Three contestants have half a minute each to perform what they think will be the music of tomorrow. Contestant Anthony Carmichael gives us England's first ever performance of rap music. The contest is judged by Look Around You's resident music expert, the Ghost of the composer Tchaikovsky, who speaks through an interpreter. We also meet the chairman of the Royal Pop and Rock Association, Sir Alan Rees, who coincidentally is the reigning French-kissing champion.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Motorcyclist Leon, 36, is airlifted to St George's after being involved in a head-on collision with a car. Graham, who is 37, has collided with another player while playing rugby and has lost feeling in his arms and legs. 4-year-old Elliott has pushed a stone up his nose to hide it from his sister.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It’s Mexican Week, and the bakers take on the sweet bread pan dulce, tackle a Mexican street food staple, and make a showstopping version of the milk-soaked tres leches cake.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Adam and Jamie revisit Archimedes's death ray with the help of the fans. They challenged the fans to make their own death rays, and invite the most promising entrants to come to M5 and compete against their own new and improved design.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Ace salvager Drew Pritchard is always on the lookout for stock for his show room. Today he visits an imposing Gothic castle in Ireland, but is there anything to buy?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Five Bells Pub in Berkshire, where entrepreneur Mark Genders hopes his guests will think his small rooms are perfectly formed. But he admits his VERY honest appraisal of his rivals' offerings may work against him.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Evadney, 56, walks into A&E after badly burning her arm when an aerosol exploded in a bonfire that she was standing close to. Sufia, who is 5, is rushed to St George's by ambulance with head and scalp injuries after she was dragged under a car while she was on her way to school. 70-year-old Pat arrives after the car that she was driving hit a wall at speed.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Electric Vehicle Charging Stations, Grappa, Lunar Rover Replicas
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Electric Vehicle Charging Stations, Grappa, and Lunar Rover Replicas and shows how each is made.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Zach, 25, is rushed to A&E after completely severing four fingers in a building site accident. Ricky, who is 85, has hurt her back after falling at home. 10-year-old Daquon was hit by a motorbike while on his way to school and has a badly broken arm.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered where your morality comes from? Is it ingrained in your brain, set by religion, or does society dictate what you deem as right or wrong? Through a series of interactive games and experiments, we'll test the strength of your moral fiber. Play along and discover how your brain makes countless moral decisions every single day. We're going to mess with your mind as we show you how outside influences like clothing, attitude, and your own evolutionary survival may sway your brain...and your morals.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day in the competition and the B&Bers face the music, as they meet to discover what they've been paid and confront their critics over their feedback.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 54-year-old Richard arrives in A&E suffering symptoms of a suspected stroke. Peter, who is 76, arrives in A&E with a severe head injury, but it's not clear what exactly has happened to him.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
At Nateby Inn in Cumbria, landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. She takes her guests to a local brewery, and the trip turns out to be a barrel of laughs when Andrew Ashmore beats Bob Osborne in a cask-rolling competition.
However, at dinner, Bob reclaims pole position in the conversation stakes when he reveals he once lived in a cave.
The following morning, it's Gaynor's turn in the spotlight and she's left fuming when guests Julia Ashley and Clare Ashmore put her through her paces with double orders for breakfast. A broken cooker means it's double trouble for Gaynor as she struggles to get out her full English.
At feedback, host Gaynor is delighted by some comments, but left disgruntled by Julia and Graham's mixed reviews.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob visit the glorious Lake District, which still has a special place in Bob’s heart thanks to his memories of teenage holidays there in the 1970s.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: Gordon tackles a pub for the first time time - The Fenwick Arms in rural Lancashire. Landlord Brian who has been running the business for over 30 years but despite his best efforts Brian and his partner Elaine are £250k in debt, losing £1,500 a week and facing bankruptcy in three months.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Maria, who is 52, suffers from MS and has been brought to resus with suspected sepsis. 6-year-old Grace has tissue stuck in her ear. Eve, 83, has fallen down the stairs to her basement.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: This time around, Adam and Jamie test a myth that footballs filled with helium will fly farther and hang longer than ones filled with regulation air. Are teeth strong enough to withstand the force of a bullet?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
Owners Graham and Julia Ashley run Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire.
The hosts treat their guests to an adrenaline-fuelled afternoon at an indoor sky diving centre, before whisking them back to the B&B for a dinner full of surprises.
The guests wake up the next day having had a faultlessly good night's sleep and looking forward to breakfast. Julia ordered two breakfasts at Gaynor Walker's B&B, so she isn't surprised when Gaynor orders off menu.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Megan, 20, is rushed to St George's after being involved in a high-speed car crash. 40-year-old Jibraeel has a cotton bud stuck in his ear. James, who is 78, has suspected internal bleeding.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
In London, artist Bob Osborne takes a laid-back approach to running his B&B, At Home, in Chiswick
On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impression of Bob's communal living space-come-mini art gallery, but on closer inspection, the guests are soon dishing the dirt.
Bob's impromptu art class gets mixed reviews and an appraisal from art critic Peter Davies on their efforts leaves some of the guests baffled. But it's at dinner when the guests are really bewildered, as Bob reveals his plans for tomorrow's breakfast.
The next morning, the artist-in-residence is a no show and the DIY breakfast seems to have almost everyone getting into the communal vibe. Unfortunately for Bob, at feedback, his guests' feelings don't make for works of art.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy sets to work getting the farm ready for its newest residents, some Sand and Black pigs. Meanwhile Kaleb is annoyed when he spots someone else drilling in his fields.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It’s Dessert Week and the bakers get all steamed up in the Signature, tackle a classic lemon meringue pie, and create a showstopping sponge cake with a hidden surprise.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Slate Tiles, Hot Dog Carts, Garage Door Openers, Bicycle Seats
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Slate Tiles, Hot Dog Carts, Garage Door Openers, and Bicycle Seats and shows how each is made.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tony, 48, and his father have been in a motorbike crash. Alison has a broken leg from a horse riding accident. Harry, who is 12, has been in a 10-bike pile-up at a velodrome.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The final visit of the week is to Chard, Somerset where Andrew and Clare Ashmore own the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse.
The competitive B&Bers have gone head to head in a whole host of activities this week, and today, Andrew and Clare hope to bowl their guests over with a game of skittles.
At dinner, the B&Bers share their first impressions of each other, with Bob and Julia offering up some highly amusing observations.
In the morning, breakfast is a lively affair with host Andrew kept on his toes by his raucous guests. In the kitchen, Clare is concerned about how Gaynor will judge their offering.
Having read their feedback, Andrew and Clare have some questions to ask on payment day.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: This is a show about your brain and money. It's present in every corner of our lives-we earn it, spend it, manage it and often obsess over it, but how well do we really understand it? If you play along you'll understand why your brain gets excited when you think you're getting a deal, why there's no such thing as a free lunch, and you'll learn how to stop paying more for less.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The build team attempts to discover electricity by flying a kite in a thunderstorm. Do innocuous legumes really increase gas? Is it possible to break wind so much that you can suffocate to death?

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Ace salvager Drew Pritchard is always on the lookout for stock for his show room. But which potential treasures will he come across today?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tim came off his bike and has suspected spinal injuries. Jeanette is struggling to breathe. Mollie, who is 91, is in A&E after her daughter accidentally crashed into a lamp post.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.sub-title: Triumph TR6, Lotus Cortina & Mini Mayfair
programme.desc: The summer brings an increase in business for Yorkshire classic car auctioneers. To keep pace, the Mathewsons introduce their new "multiple classic collection plan", much to the amusement of office manager Sarah. Dave sets off to collect a Triumph TR6, a Lotus Cortina and a Mini Mayfair wedding car and bring them back to the auction house. The team heads to The Wirral for a rare survivor, a DAF 33 from the ‘70s. "It's a car that had a bit of an image issue back in the day, but it also had a gearbox ahead of its time," says Derek - as everyone tries to explain how it works before the car is auctioned off.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
It's time for the B&B-ers to face the music, as they come together for one last time to find out what they've been paid and confront their critics, face to face.
Nateby Inn is first up and host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs.
Next, conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm owners Graham and Julia tackle Gaynor's complaints, and Georgie steps in to the hosts' defence.
Artist Bob Osborne has a list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her comments come back to haunt her. Tension runs high as everyone finds out what they've been paid, but who will be crowned best value B&B of the week? It's a close call.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a fifty-six year old Citroën 2CV, retired in the sunny south of France. It’s a real people’s car with bags of charm. With cheeky bug-eyed headlights and rare suicide doors, the 2CV is one of the most recognisable cars ever designed and renowned, over its forty-two year production cycle, for its stark simplicity. Almost every aspect of this quirky car requires attention; Edd strips it back to bare bones to breathe new life back into the smoky old engine. A fresh paint job and interior will help to secure the car’s future for another fifty years but can Mike get top dollar for a car with no frills?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Hussnain is rushed to A&E after being caught up in a accident involving a police car chasing another vehicle. Wrestler Kieran comes in after injuring his knee in a bout.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, 51, is brought to resus after coming off his bike. Dibs, who is 60, has fallen 10 feet off a balcony onto concrete. 10-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A new competition kicks off at Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where Ian Sugden and his wife Sam treat their guests to an afternoon on the firing range and fancy bacon for breakfast.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: This episode deals with savanna, steppe, tundra, prairie, and looks at the importance and resilience of grasses in such treeless ecosystems. Their vast expanses contain the largest concentration of animal life. Over Africa's savanna, a swarm of 1.5 billion red-billed queleas are caught on camera, the largest flock of birds ever depicted.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: If you fill a normal raft with helium can you fly? Tory and Kari tackle a conspiracy theory familiar to many frequent flyers. Can your cell phone interfere with a plane's instruments?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Halloween has come early as the remaining bakers tackle three terrifying challenges. There's a seasonal Signature in the form of an apple cake, followed by a s'more-ish Technical and, in the Showstopper, they create a Halloween piñata.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Judy, who is 72, is airlifted to St George's after being trampled by a herd of cows while she was walking her dog. Tamzen, who is 13, has broken her leg at trampoline practice at school. 18-year-old art student Hermione has cut her hand on a glass at her part-time waitressing job.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
Lisa Holloway prides herself on providing a home from home for her guests. Sadly, Sam and Ian Sugden are disappointed by the dust, while Dave and Jo find Lisa's prices hard to swallow.
Next morning at breakfast, retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell are heard openly criticising the haddock.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Racing Leathers, Evaporative Cooling Towers, Wood Rocking Chairs, Wire Wheels
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Racing Leathers, Evaporative Cooling Towers, Wood Rocking Chairs, and Wire Wheels and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
At the White Horse pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, Dave and Jo Bate hope the guests will embrace the pub vibe and an afternoon of Morris dancing in front of the ancient cathedral.
But what do they make of Dave's revelations at dinner concerning his 'big one', let alone 'tomatogate' at breakfast the next morning?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 3-year-old Clara has a chickpea stuck up her nose. Danny has fallen 20 feet through a skylight. Tara has severe abdominal pains.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: Whether you're a skeptic or a believer in the supernatural, you have to admit there are things out there your brain just can't quite explain. Get ready for a series of games and experiments designed to test your brain's sixth sense. If you play along, you'll discover that your brain actually knows more about the unknown than you might think.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Richard, who's 47, has fallen off a ladder while he was fixing his roof. 87-year-old Doreen arrives in resus suffering from a serious infection. 5-year-old Cojo has come to paediatrics after injuring his elbow while he was wrestling with his brother.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob take to the water in the Norfolk Broads to fish for rudd, full of the joys of the first day of the fishing season.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
In Cheshire, retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell shock some of their guests by offering only a shared bathroom.
But that's nothing compared to Sam and Ian Sugden's reaction to what they find lurking beneath their bed.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: John has been in a cycling accident and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney has severe chest pains. Alan has fallen and fractured his elbow.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: Adam and Jamie attempt to solve another high calibre conundrum to see if a celebratory gun fire can kill when the bullets fall back to earth. Meanwhile the other members shake their thirst on another round of Vodka myths.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc:
In this third helping, Gordon finds a chef caught in a time warp when he tackles a picturesque but largely unfrequented upmarket restaurant in King's Lynn, a traditional market town on the Norfolk coast. For the last eighteen months, ex-Michelin-starred chef, Nick, has owned and run Rococo but his past success is now eluding him. The menu is past its sell-by date, the service is stuffy, the food is over-priced and the only thing being fed is his ego.
Yet, despite debts of £100k and the prospect of putting his home at risk and his young family on the streets, Nick continues to cook comatose in a 1990s fantasy land of his own making. Gordon forces him to face his failures, strips down the menu and dismantles the claustrophobic dining room.
But when he discovers the depths of Nick's stubbornness - and that he's locked him out of the restaurant (a Kitchen Nightmares' first), even Gordon is forced to face the prospect of failure. Will Nick ever get up to speed and recapture his glory days, or will he carry on sleep-walking his way to disaster?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&Bers gather for one last time to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences. And a heated exchange about cleaning leads to tears.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Mountain Bikes, Rice, Lever Action Rifles
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Mountain Bikes, Rice, and Lever Action Rifles and shows how each is made.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: It's Christmas time on the farm and Kaleb gives Jeremy a festive surprise. However their relationship becomes strained as they bicker while getting on with the farms winter jobs.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Lillian has extremely low blood pressure, and doctors discover that she is in the latter stages of pancreatic cancer. Carolyn bruised her neck and face when she slipped on wet decking. Dennis is having difficulty breathing.

programme.title: Look Around You
programme.desc: The team looks at advances in healthcare, and Jack Morgan goes under the knife as Dr Fu supervizes the efforts of a robot-surgeon called Medibot to create the perfect face. Other presenters try out a new toothbrush that burns away plaque - though with one rather serious side effect... And in a special report, the team visits a Sleep Institute and meets its director, Professor Craven, a man with some cutting-edge ideas.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It’s Custard Week and the bakers put their twist on floating islands in the signature, tackle a summer staple, and make custard the star and basis of a showstopping set gateau.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
On the Isle of Man, owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace the green ethos of Knockaloe Beg farm.
During an afternoon of laser mayhem, the Isle of Man residents battle the mainlanders, and two guests prove they're the life and soul of the party.
The next day, after breakfast, Fiona introduces some farmyard fun as the guests head out for some hands-on animal antics.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Psychiatric nurse Christie has severe burns after being attacked by a patient. Joe's cousin accidentally stabbed him in the thigh. 2-year-old Freddie will not let doctors look in his eye after complaining of pain.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The second visit is to The Arches on the Isle of Man where ex-demolition man Phil Routledge is proud of his super-modern B&B, which was voted the best in the Isle of Man.
On arrival, his guests are blown away by the views, and fellow Isle of Man residents John and Fiona Anderson lap up the his and hers bath. But the lack of instructions for the state-of-the art gadgetry leaves guest Julie Brogan stumped.
Phil wins over his guests after an afternoon of scuba diving in the Arches' own pool, and in the morning, Phil's wife Jill takes charge in the kitchen where some 'perfect' eggs Benedict puts the pressure on the mainlanders who are hosting next.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: 48-year-old paraglider pilot Martin is airlifted to St George's after a gust of wind threw him into a cliff. Eddie has been weakened by chemotherapy and has severe sepsis. Graham's nose will not stop bleeding.

programme.title: Brain Games
programme.desc: In this episode about memory, we're going to show you what memory is, why you even have one, and why you keep forgetting where you put those darn keys! And if you play along you'll see why forgetting can actually be a good thing! It's going to be a night to remember...on Brain Games.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: A look back at myths that generated a truck load of angry fan mail. In Split Arrow, the 3 Mythkateers take another aim at Robin Hood's arrow. The Confederate Rocket will be tested with a Salami launch. Another shot at Bullet-Proof water.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The third visit is to The Trafford Hotel in Blackpool. Owners Robbie and John Welling are proud of their traditional bucket and spade B&B, and on arrival, the guests are pleasantly surprised.
The hosts want to sweeten the deal with an afternoon learning the art of making Blackpool rock, and the competitive juices get flowing as the Isle of Man B&Bs take on the mainland B&Bs.
Next morning, Robbie and John present Fiona and John with a full English made entirely of Blackpool rock but it's not enough to distract Fiona from commenting on the mass market ingredients in the real breakfast. At check-out, the hosts are left wondering why Phil says he won't return again.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Zac, who is 30, has dislocated and fractured his ankle playing football and doctors rush to restore the blood flow to his foot. 8-year-old Jayden has a swollen eye after playing in a shed. Ian, 52, has fallen off a lorry tailgate and has a suspected broken back and pelvis.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc:
The last visit of the week is to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire where empty-nester Julie loves to fill her manor house with people - as long as they don't order poached eggs.
Julie's rivals are gobsmacked by their surroundings, but Robbie and John Welling aren't thrilled with the lack of en-suite and old blu tack on their bedroom walls, while John and Fiona are shocked to find synthetic bedding.
With rain stopping play outside, Julie decides on some old fashioned parlour games - so a fiercely fought session of hide and seek ensues.
In the morning, Julie's forced to cook the dreaded poached eggs, and, as she panics, some of the guests think that she's too apologetic. Come check-out, some glowing feedback leaves Julie in tears but she believes the competition is too close to call.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: This episode demonstrates the production processes for Saunas, Wheelchair Lifts, and Dioramas and shows how each is made.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Glyn is brought in after his leg is run over by a three tonne digger. John, who is 93 and has severe sepsis, is admitted after a fall at home. A toddler is brought in with diarrhoea.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian wins a boat on "The Price is Right" and decides to sail to Texas to help women seeking legal abortions.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire admits that he is in love with Lois. Unfortunately, he proclaims his love while he has accidentally pocket-dialed Peter. Peter is furious and Brian takes it upon himself to fix the situation.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Take This Job and Love It
programme.desc: Peter and his friends wonder aloud about what their dream jobs would be.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a yacht race that the Griffins win through disobeying Lois' father, Lois decides to fulfill her dream of being a model, which her father never allowed. After she begins partying and using drugs, it is up to Peter and Lois' father to bring her back.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Screams of Silence: The Story of Brenda Q
programme.desc: Quagmire lands in the hospital and his sister, Brenda, and her boyfriend, Jeff, rush to Quahog to nurse him back to health. As Glenn recovers he quickly realizes that Brenda is the one who needs help.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter accidentally discovers a missing child, he is hailed as town hero. Meanwhile, Joe quits his job and heads to Niagara Falls to live life to the fullest.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris finish last in a "Generation Gap" game at a carnival, forcing them to try to reconnect. Brian starts to date a carny, whom he can't break up with even after witnessing some of her crude habits.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: One Foot in Front of the Mother
programme.desc: Due to a police complaint about his excessive barking, Brian has to wear a shock collar; looking to join an after-school team, Chris discovers a talent for speed walking.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter calls out the name "Francis" while making love with Lois, but she believes he meant his deceased father, leading to a round of therapy. Things worsen when Lois discovers "Frances" is the name of the Clam's new, pretty waitress.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg has fun partying with Peter when he takes her to a college for an interview. Brian must learn how to live in the wild when he gets sprayed by a skunk and is prohibited from entering the house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Hard Times at Adam West High
programme.desc: Chris uses Stewie's time machine to stop himself from having an erection during a book report. Peter becomes jealous when he sits out of a lottery pool and the guys win.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois embarks on a journey of self-discovery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland returns to town, but is instantly banned from spending time with Peter when Lois and Donna get into a big fight over parenting.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Stewie finally understand each other - thanks to Brian's edibles - and try to understand their complicated relationship.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's marriage to Jess doesn't go as planned, especially after her miraculous cancer recovery, so at Peter's suggestion, Brian lets himself go, leading Jess to make a surprising decision.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois joins Chris's school as a substitute teacher, launching him into the cool kids club... but only time will tell how long he remains a member.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this follow up to the "Viewer Mail" episodes, the Griffin family retells The Lord of the Rings, explains how the Griffins' ancestors arrived in Quahog, and looks back at Quagmire's 1960s variety show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: On the search to find a new hobby, Chris meets a puppeteer named Franz, an old friend of Herbert's. Despite Herbert's warnings, Chris and Franz remain friends until Franz's checkered past is suddenly revealed.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Principal Shepherd moves in with the Griffins after he is fired for fat-shaming Chris at school. Meanwhile, Brian sues Stewie for writing a series of children's books which portray him as an idiot.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter makes it through a bad flu season without getting sick, the hospital asks him to volunteer as a nurse, but he finds himself at a crossroads when his mortal enemy, The Giant Chicken, is admitted to the ER. Meanwhile, Brian receives his genetic test results and learns he is part cat, causing him to have an identity crisis.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter learns from a psychic through a palm reading that he had a fascinating past life in the 17th century as Griffin Peterson, a dignified English gentleman who vied for the love of Lady Redbush in the newly founded American colony of Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter and the guys get caught pretending to be military veterans and are found guilty of the Stolen Valor Act, a judge sentences them to join the U.S. Coast Guard.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian discovers his nose can detect diseases and smells that someone in the Griffin house has cancer.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter injures himself and a family of squirrels in a chainsaw accident, Chris and Stewie take in one of the injured rodents to nurse it back to health. Meanwhile, a chiropractor fixes Peter's injury by realigning his spine - and restoring him to his "true height" of 6'5".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After discovering his checks are actually a large size post-it pad, Peter demands he have access to the checking account. While at the bank, Peter & Lois discover that his first name is actually Justin, so Peter embraces his new partying “bro” lifestyle. Meanwhile, Joe arrests Glenn after he sleeps with an underage girl, which leads to an unexpected reunion.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's future life could not be going any better, living as a wealthy, old Mr. Burns-type man, until his retirement is interrupted by Brian, who brings news that Peter is dying.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois receives a surprising birthday present from her father, Carter Pewterschmidt, that leads to an unfortunate accident leaving him dependent on Lois and the Griffin's care.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets a job as the new night watchman, he and the guys turn Pawtucket Brewery into an after-hours bar. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian's plan to collect "lost dog" return money backfires when Brian is used for a dangerous TikTok stunt.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian is smitten with a female show dog and enters a show dog competition in which the winner gets to breed her; Peter and Lois go to extreme measures to try to convince Chris that Arthur Valentine doesn't exist.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Return of the King (of Queens)
programme.desc: Lois decides to sell the family's television to help pay for Christmas presents, but when Kevin James gets upset over losing Peter as a viewer, he apparates into the Griffin house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian form a band aimed at children, but face a breakup when they set their eyes on the same girl. Meanwhile, Chris gets a job as Quagmire's personal assistant for his sex life.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Mort bowls a perfect game, and Quagmire gets a key to the city by performing CPR, Peter notices he has nothing to be proud of. He then attempts to achieve fame by setting the world's record for most nickels swallowed. However, a slight miscalculation leaves him blind with nickel poisoning.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter, Cleveland and Joe come across a VHS tape called “Winter/Summer” an Asian soap opera that Quagmire starred in when he was living in Korea. Unable to find the final episode of the series, the guys travel to Korea to find it and Quagmire is reunited with his old lover. Meanwhile, Stewie worries he might become a redhead.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter fills in for Santa at the mall, but lets the power go to his head when he realises just what he can get away with. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian become holiday office-party crashers.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To restore his public image, Carter adopts a young girl from an orphanage, drawing jealousy from Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter, Quagmire and Joe visit the Harvard University campus, Quagmire accidentally marries a prostitute. In order to get out of his marriage, Quagmire pretends to be lovers with Peter.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter’s sister, Karen, decides to come for Thanksgiving and bullies Peter the same way Meg treats him. While Meg is at first amused, she later feels badly and decides to help Peter train to defeat his sister in a wrestling match. Meanwhile, to avoid overeating on Thanksgiving, Stewie and Brian decide to go on a cleanse, which Stewie takes one step too far.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins provide DVD commentary on a recent episode and reveal never-before-told drama between Peter and Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends are at the record store and they tell the stories of three rock 'n' roll singers: Jim Morrison, who was the vocalist for The Doors, an American rock band and Jim's sudden downfall. Muddy Waters, an American blues singer, but his credit for his music doesn't get noticed. Elton John, a British singer and pianist, who has a hard time finding acceptance and he learns that seeking his parent's love is meaningless.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Overwhelmed with financial troubles, Mort asks Peter and Quagmire to help him save his pharmacy. However, things take a turn for the worse when the trio decides to burn it down to collect insurance money

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland is fired as a mailman after an ignominious mishap, but flourishes in his new job at the brewery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's non-stop sweating becomes a liability at his company, so his boss tells him to work from home. Meanwhile, after Stewie and Chris cause thousands of dollars in damage to an electronics store, the two create a fake Kickstarter campaign with Brian to pay back the money.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg plans her wedding to an unexpected Quahog resident. Meanwhile, Peter finds a new vest and on one of many adventures fitting for his new attire, his arms are torn off and he is left with tiny hands that have yet to grow back.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A powerful sleeping aid medication gives Joe the ability to walk while he is asleep, but Joe grows concerned when it becomes clear that the guys prefer his sleepwalking self.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg discovers her tears produce delicious cookies, and her, along with her siblings, enjoy a profit until the success makes her too happy to cry. Meanwhile, Peter and the guys launch a "male talk show."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg agrees to be Bruce and Jeffery's surrogate causing a pregnant Meg to annoy her family. But, when the couple fails to pick up the baby, the Griffins must take care of their new family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help Chris ace his ninth grade history class, Stewie and Brian employ the time travel machine for educational purposes. Not surprisingly, all three get stuck in the past.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter helps Joe fulfill his dream of getting a book published, only to take over as the writer. In the meantime, Brian develops an extreme thirst for exercise.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian are detectives who embark on solving a string of mysterious murders set in Victorian-era London.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A new boss quells Peter’s favorite part about work: office birthdays. Stewie is put in detention and comes out a ruffian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian becomes romantically interested in someone and it inspires him to use Stewie's time machine. Strange consequences result from their journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Peter are divided by differing views on who the next Mayor of Quahog should be. Upset at Lois' first choice, Peter seeks out the late Mayor Adam West's cousin, Wild Wild West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter secretly gets a credit card and overspends the family money, so Lois grounds him. To plan the perfect escape, he builds a tunnel leading directly to The Drunken Clam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's cosmetic surgery leads him down a new career path as a real estate agent, which gets him into trouble with Quagmire.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg starts dating a chicken she meets on a reality dating show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the Griffins win a trip to Maine at Peter's company picnic, they decide to invite their friends on the vacation. Tensions run high and things soon spiral out of control when the cabin burns down.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Winner of a Snapple contest, Lois uses her winnings to take Meg on a tropical vacation. Flaunting their wealth, the two are kidnapped. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian undergo "friendship therapy."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie is surprised when he makes friends with another boy his age, but later taken aback when he learns that Stewie wasn't invited to his birthday party.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes friends with a cool, handsome paramedic named Stryker Foxx. Wrapped up in his newfound "coolness," he soon dumps Quagmire, Joe and Cleveland.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg pursues an unlikely friendship with an NFL great; Stewie and Brian host a dinner party at a home furnishing store.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In search of Stewie's cherished big wheel tricycle, that Peter threw out, Stewie and Brian embark on an adventure to "Garbage Island," a giant floating mass of trash out in the sea. Meanwhile, Lois tries to help Quagmire elevate his dating game, as he courts a middle-aged woman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe accidentally sees Lois naked, leading to a reinvigoration of his sex life with Bonnie. Meanwhile, Stewie is gifted an electric SUV and quickly embodies the "Range Rover" lifestyle.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg begins working at a retirement community and starts stealing from the residents. Then Chris finds out and wants in on the heist. When the residents catch them, they blackmail them and force the thieves to listen to all of their boring stories.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter must gain his family's respect back after he makes a humiliating throw. Brian and Stewie become chiropractors.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets carried away when the Nielsen people select the Griffins to have their television-viewing habits monitored and he steals a number of the Nielsen boxes in hopes of controlling the airwaves.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide to open a cookie store, but Peter ruins things when he decides to bring clients in his own way.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Tired of not winning an Emmy, Peter embarks on his own For Your Consideration campaign for Family Guy by making the show more like Emmy-winning shows - and no category is off limits.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris leaves home to move in with Herbert while Stewie and Brian become obsessed with singer Anne Murray.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie designs a "Terminator" Peter robot to destroy Lois for trying to feed him broccoli, but when things don't go according to plan, Stewie and Brian must go on a Terminator-esque mission to save Stewie's life.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter tries to protest Hollywood after learning that Brad Pitt was cast in a biopic about a politician. After Pitt injures himself on set, Peter must act out the role himself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois vacation in Florida, only to discover they've rented a condo in a retirement community. Back at home, Brian helps Stewie take his first shower.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Death grants Peter's wish to go back in time to 1984 when he was 18, but only for one night. He ends up bailing on a date with Lois to hang out with a movie actress. When he returns to the present, he soon realizes that everything is anything but normal, as he is now married to the former actress, and Lois is with Quagmire. Meanwhile, Stewie, Chris and Meg have also taken on Quagmire's charming characteristics.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Impelled by nostalgia, Peter recreates movie scenes from his favorite decade; Stewie and Doug compete to retrieve a Frisbee stuck at the top of the jungle gym.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie becomes engaged to Rupert, but Brian is upset when he is not chosen as the best man.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg is erroneously reported to have died in a car crash, she goes along with the story to start a new life elsewhere. Meanwhile, the Griffins experience a life without Meg Griffin.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Vladimir Putin comes to Quahog after Peter writes to him about an alternate ending to the Russian version of Rocky IV (1985).

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this Griffin-family twist on the popular HBO series, Peter suspects Lois of betraying his trust with Quagmire at a swanky vacation, while Stewie worries that his wealthy new friends plan to sacrifice him in a volcano.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After discovering his checks are actually a large size post-it pad, Peter demands he have access to the checking account. While at the bank, Peter & Lois discover that his first name is actually Justin, so Peter embraces his new partying “bro” lifestyle. Meanwhile, Joe arrests Glenn after he sleeps with an underage girl, which leads to an unexpected reunion.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Lois reads a new book on the joys of decluttering, she challenges the rest of the Griffins to throw anything out of the house that does not spark joy. While all starts out well, Lois soon takes the decluttering to a surprising extreme.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian's Instagram is hacked, Meg takes Stewie and Brian to Russia, only to realize she'd rather stay there. Meanwhile, Lois reverts to her tormented teenage self when Quagmire starts dating her high school bully.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois vacation in Florida, only to discover they've rented a condo in a retirement community. Back at home, Brian helps Stewie take his first shower.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: At odds with Peter, Lois takes Quagmire to her high school reunion; the rest of the family must cover up the death of a pizza delivery man.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland is fired as a mailman after an ignominious mishap, but flourishes in his new job at the brewery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Dissatisfied with his current love life, Brian decides to slow things down when he meets the girl of his dreams, Carolyn. However, Cleveland soon sweeps Carolyn off her feet, and Brian must do anything he can do, to win her back, even if it means involving Cleveland's ex-wife, Loretta.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois gets a job as a music teacher at Meg and Chris' school , but when she accidentally gets Connie D'Mico expelled, Connie keeps pranking Lois until she threatens her at the supermarket. Later, when Connie is reported to be murdered, Lois is framed and arrested for her murder.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: One mini-episode introduces a British version of the Griffins. A comedian takes over Quahog in another. And a third views the world from Stewie's POV.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie is diagnosed with ADD and prescribed Adderall, which Brian takes to write a 2000-page proposal to pitch to author George R.R. Martin at a book convention. Meanwhile, Quagmire and Peter compete, Iron Chef-style, to determine which of them gets to keep his new cooking show.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg deals with a college admissions scandal, while Brian embarks on a fitness journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter’s couch is stolen from his front lawn, the guys form a neighborhood watch group. While on duty, Peter sees someone trying to climb into Cleveland’s house and he shoots him. Peter is then arrested and charged with a hate crime. Meanwhile, Cleveland is touched when Peter finally admits to the accident, so he takes the fall for the crime and Peter is exonerated.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg gets a new job at the airport, where she's instantly popular for being the hottest employee there.
