Meanwhile, Cleveland becomes a therapist and starts giving Peter and Lois relationship advice, but Peter quickly becomes frustrated and threatens to tell Donna a big secret about what went down at Cleveland's bachelor party.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter helps Joe fulfill his dream of getting a book published, only to take over as the writer. In the meantime, Brian develops an extreme thirst for exercise.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Mort bowls a perfect game, and Quagmire gets a key to the city by performing CPR, Peter notices he has nothing to be proud of. He then attempts to achieve fame by setting the world's record for most nickels swallowed. However, a slight miscalculation leaves him blind with nickel poisoning.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg is thrilled to be recruited as a model, but it turns out to be for a very specific type of modeling: the foot-fetish industry. Meanwhile, Brian accompanies Stewie on an open-road adventure when Stewie decides he wants to experience life outside of preschool.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins spoof "Only Murders in the Building," "The Dropout," and "The Bear."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After attending a baseball game, Peter and Chris are inspired to start a memorabilia business. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to combat aging by getting plastic surgery, but ends up taking it too far.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie finds himself in public disgrace after a popstar unleashes her fan base on him for a benign comment. Meanwhile, Peter gets lap band surgery and enjoys the merriment of excess skin.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Screams of Silence: The Story of Brenda Q
programme.desc: Quagmire lands in the hospital and his sister, Brenda, and her boyfriend, Jeff, rush to Quahog to nurse him back to health. As Glenn recovers he quickly realizes that Brenda is the one who needs help.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Overwhelmed with financial troubles, Mort asks Peter and Quagmire to help him save his pharmacy. However, things take a turn for the worse when the trio decides to burn it down to collect insurance money

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie builds a DNA-altering machine that will allow him to remain smart forever, but the invention has the adverse effect and he instead transforms into what he should have always been - an actual baby.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: An accident leaves Peter with a high-pitched voice, so Jesus coaches him into fending off the choir boys; Stewie is attracted to Lois' new masculine physique.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter secretly gets a credit card and overspends the family money, so Lois grounds him. To plan the perfect escape, he builds a tunnel leading directly to The Drunken Clam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter calls out the name "Francis" while making love with Lois, but she believes he meant his deceased father, leading to a round of therapy. Things worsen when Lois discovers "Frances" is the name of the Clam's new, pretty waitress.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois celebrate their anniversary, but it goes south when Lois falls victim to a humiliating prank. Peter starts to feel emasculated for his actions and decides to prove to Lois that he is still the alpha. Meanwhile, Stewie finds a mini-fridge and starts to live the dorm room lifestyle, much to Brian's dismay.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie's future life could not be going any better, living as a wealthy, old Mr. Burns-type man, until his retirement is interrupted by Brian, who brings news that Peter is dying.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To restore his public image, Carter adopts a young girl from an orphanage, drawing jealousy from Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois accidentally set up a cutaway gag together, which somehow transports them into the cutaway itself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a Christmas tree lightning ceremony Brian crashes into Mayor West's nativity scene causing outrage.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris runs away to South America after a school hazing during his first day of being a freshman. Meanwhile, Peter gets a job at the brewery, where he is allowed to drink on the job, just as long he controls himself. However, Peter's drinking habit gets out of hand, Angela demotes Peter to work at shipping.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets a job as the new night watchman, he and the guys turn Pawtucket Brewery into an after-hours bar. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian's plan to collect "lost dog" return money backfires when Brian is used for a dangerous TikTok stunt.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie becomes obsessed with Lois, after she saves Rupert. Though Lois doesn't take to the new Stewie all too well. Meanwhile, Peter thinks his doctor is molesting him, when he undergoes a prostate exam.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: On the night before Thanksgiving, Brian and Peter drunkenly devour the holiday bird, and then must go out to find a replacement. In Peter absence, Chris attempts to step up and become the man of the house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Quagmire opens a cat café in town, Brian decides to take a stand to get the café shut down. Meanwhile, when Meg and Chris get in trouble at school, Lois becomes nervous and decides they need to go to church camp in order to find their way.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When their bank accounts begin to run dry, the Griffins look for ways to make money. Stewie gets cast in a peanut butter commercial and becomes the money maker for the family. Meanwhile, Lois and Peter become crazy show business parents and Brian helps Stewie realize that he does not want to be a child actor.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian tries to use Dylan's television connections to further his career.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie finally says his first word, and it's a swear. When Lois is shunned by the other moms, she tries to figure out where Stewie could have possibly learned it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: "Family Guy" retells three Oscar-winning stories: "Silence of the Lambs," "American Beauty" and "Forrest Gump".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie becomes engaged to Rupert, but Brian is upset when he is not chosen as the best man.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After feeling unappreciated by her family, Lois taps into her villainous side and attempts to win "Best Customer" at her favorite coffee shop.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's non-stop sweating becomes a liability at his company, so his boss tells him to work from home. Meanwhile, after Stewie and Chris cause thousands of dollars in damage to an electronics store, the two create a fake Kickstarter campaign with Brian to pay back the money.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian adopted a cat and introduced to Family. Meg tries to warn them Pouncey is an evil cat but no one believes her. Glenn tells Meg about that cat and her past. Meanwhile Pouncey puts Stewie and Brian under her and plans to take them away forever.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: You Can't Do That on Television, Peter
programme.desc: When Peter's favorite kiddie show gets canceled, he decides to create and star in his own. Meanwhile, Meg starts an internship for Dr. Hartman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Mayor West marries Lois’ sister, Carol, they move into the Griffin’s house to be one big “happy” family. To make room for everyone, Stewie is bumped out of his room and takes over Chris’ room, but not everyone is annoyed by the change. Peter and Mayor West bond and become best friends.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After teddy bear Rupert gets recalled, Stewie stops at nothing to get back his beloved best friend. Meanwhile, When Peter falls ill and can't compete in his bowling tournament, Peter becomes jealous when Lois fills in for him.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian falls in love with a tech support worker in India, so he and Stewie travel there to find her. Meanwhile, Joe invites Peter to his bingo night, but when Peter becomes the new center of attention, Lois helps Joe reclaim the group.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian quickly falls in love with a woman, and after receiving devastating news regarding her cancer diagnosis, he proposes marriage.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter, Cleveland and Joe come across a VHS tape called “Winter/Summer” an Asian soap opera that Quagmire starred in when he was living in Korea. Unable to find the final episode of the series, the guys travel to Korea to find it and Quagmire is reunited with his old lover. Meanwhile, Stewie worries he might become a redhead.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie receives a participation medal in his school's toddler games, he begins to question everything he has ever won. Meanwhile, Peter causes a fight between Lois' parents and must repair the damage he caused.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The network decides that it's time to explore some unique ways to reboot the series. When three versions of the reboots are shown to a focus group, the feedback takes some surprising turns.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's cosmetic surgery leads him down a new career path as a real estate agent, which gets him into trouble with Quagmire.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Principal Shepherd moves in with the Griffins after he is fired for fat-shaming Chris at school. Meanwhile, Brian sues Stewie for writing a series of children's books which portray him as an idiot.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Mayor West raises the drinking age to 50. Because of dog years, Brian is the only one old enough to buy alcohol, so Peter makes him buy all of his booze.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg finally turns 18, Quagmire seizes the opportunity to pursue his next romantic conquest. As the new couple's relationship blossoms, Peter stops at nothing to come in between his innocent - well, somewhat innocent - daughter and his creepy friend.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian go back in time to stop Bertram from killing Leonardo da Vinci in order to erase Stewie from the universe.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg begins working at a retirement community and starts stealing from the residents. Then Chris finds out and wants in on the heist. When the residents catch them, they blackmail them and force the thieves to listen to all of their boring stories.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter and Quagmire accidentally switch underwear, they begin to mimic the other's behavior. Stewie and Doug agree to commit each other's murders.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: During a game of "hide and seek," Stewie takes refuge in Quagmire's suitcase, only to wind up in Paris. Meanwhile, Peter and Lois take a getaway to Vermont, where Lois is intent on breaking Bonnie's Instagram "like" record.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris finish last in a "Generation Gap" game at a carnival, forcing them to try to reconnect. Brian starts to date a carny, whom he can't break up with even after witnessing some of her crude habits.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's marriage to Jess doesn't go as planned, especially after her miraculous cancer recovery, so at Peter's suggestion, Brian lets himself go, leading Jess to make a surprising decision.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistakenly identified as transgender, which leads to an unexpected event and an important learning lesson.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris go to fat camp where they cross paths with a serial killer who targets overweight kids; an album cover frightens Stewie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this three-act parody of prominent historical eras, the Griffins take a humorous look at The French Revolution, the American Civil War, and World War II.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Peter are mistaken for "young parents" by another couple at Stewie's school and invited to live in a millennial apartment complex, leaving Meg and Chris home alone.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends are at the record store and they tell the stories of three rock 'n' roll singers: Jim Morrison, who was the vocalist for The Doors, an American rock band and Jim's sudden downfall. Muddy Waters, an American blues singer, but his credit for his music doesn't get noticed. Elton John, a British singer and pianist, who has a hard time finding acceptance and he learns that seeking his parent's love is meaningless.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide to go on a second honeymoon. But when Peter crashes the car, he decides to pose as Mel Gibson to stay in his fancy hotel suite, where they find a copy of a "The Passion of the Christ" sequel. They attempt to destroy the film and end up being chased by two priests who went to retrieve the film.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie designs a "Terminator" Peter robot to destroy Lois for trying to feed him broccoli, but when things don't go according to plan, Stewie and Brian must go on a Terminator-esque mission to save Stewie's life.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois receives a surprising birthday present from her father, Carter Pewterschmidt, that leads to an unfortunate accident leaving him dependent on Lois and the Griffin's care.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter grows frustrated when players on opposing teams continue to thank God after defeating his beloved New England Patriots football team, so he and the guys embark on a global quest to find God and ask Him to stop interfering with the outcome of professional football games.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie takes drastic measures when he thinks he is diagnosed with terminal cooties. Peter becomes infatuated with Bonnie's cooking.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian bonds with his girlfriend's son, both Brian and Kyle become fast friends after he defends the young boy from bullies. Peter, Quagmire, and Cleveland become intrigued with Joe's new classic corvette, they take a joy ride in the car without Joe's consent.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Return of the King (of Queens)
programme.desc: Lois decides to sell the family's television to help pay for Christmas presents, but when Kevin James gets upset over losing Peter as a viewer, he apparates into the Griffin house.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter starts coaching for Quahog's minor league baseball team, he gets constipated during the national anthem and must kneel, unintentionally becoming the face of racial justice.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys' physical prowess is tested at Mayor West's dude ranch. Meanwhile, Stewie thinks he's gotten his first period.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: FAMILY GUY is reimagined as a series that’s been on the air for 60 years and, in a special retrospective, it looks back at the cultural events and issues that were tackled on the show in the 1950s, '60s and '70s.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Brian accidentally becomes a local hero, an old street pal, and now a successful millionaire dog, reaches out and tries to convince him to get neutered. Meanwhile, Lois gets a pixie cut against Peter's wishes, so Peter retaliates by trying to out-embarrass her.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois books a fun couple’s vacation in the Bahamas. But, much to Peter’s surprise, it turns out to be a marriage counseling program instead. Meanwhile, Carter watches the kids at home and becomes frustrated that they’re too into their electronics to play games the old-fashioned way. So he tries to fix it himself.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian are detectives who embark on solving a string of mysterious murders set in Victorian-era London.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a vestigial twin grows out of Peter's neck, which he names Chip, he eventually starts realizing that people like Chip more than they like him, so Peter has him amputated. However, after Peter gets into trouble, he realizes he needs Chip more than ever.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To boost Chris' self-esteem, Peter and Lois send him to space camp. When camp ends and the Griffins come to pick Chris up, they accidentally are launched into outer space, and it's up to Chris to land the clan safely back on earth.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her father attend the funeral of Lois' childhood nanny. Peter and the rest of the family turn Pewterschmidt mansion into a hotel.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Quagmire starts dating a mystery woman, Peter, Joe and Cleveland go on a stakeout and discover her to be the librarian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian and Peter are required to attend a 12-step program to deal with their drinking problems; Death gives Peter a glimpse of what life would be like without alcohol.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: A colonoscopy reveals Peter has a rare collectible toy action figure lodged in his intestine, but the reward for selling it causes a rift between him and the guys.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Brian’s friendship becomes strained after Brian begins dating one of Meg’s high school teachers. Peter finds a replacement friend in James Woods, who is visiting Quahog because of a suggestion to rename James Woods High after Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Meg saves Stewie from a choking incident, Stewie makes it his personal mission to create a better life for Meg. Meanwhile, Chris is sent to a vocational school.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: It’s winter in Quahog, and Lois’ parents take Stewie away on a trip. But when Carter falls asleep at the wheel, the family decides it might be time for him to retire. Peter and Lois take the clan to Florida to check out some fine retirement communities, but Carter refuses to go down without a fight.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and the guys take a stand against their wives and flee to the Deep South on a road trip. On their Deep South road trip, Peter and the guys earn a one-way ticket to jail, then decide to make a run for it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter loses the Griffins' home in a reverse mortgage, he and Mayor West travel to confront the man advertising the scheme, Tom Selleck. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian open a booth at the flea market, leading to a rivalry with Bruce.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter talks Joe into having an affair and gets into another epic fight with the giant chicken.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris receives a special one-dollar bill as a birthday present, but he quickly loses it and the dollar passes into the possession of everybody in Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois joins Chris's school as a substitute teacher, launching him into the cool kids club... but only time will tell how long he remains a member.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: This "Top Gun" parody chronicles Peter/Maverick getting together with Lois/Charlie, the tragic death of Joe/Goose, and Peter/Maverick's blooming relationship with Chris/Rooster.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Stewie finally understand each other - thanks to Brian's edibles - and try to understand their complicated relationship.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg plans her wedding to an unexpected Quahog resident. Meanwhile, Peter finds a new vest and on one of many adventures fitting for his new attire, his arms are torn off and he is left with tiny hands that have yet to grow back.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the brewery announces it will be firing one employee, Peter is nervous he will be the one let go; the family departs on a Yacht Rock cruise that doesn't go as expected.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie starts going to the gym with Peter after he gets beaten up by Joe's baby daughter Susie, but just when it seems that his hard work is paying off, Peter reveals that he's been giving him anabolic steroid. Meanwhile, Chris begins dating a popular girl at school, only to see her popularity subside.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: 8 Simple Rules for Buying My Teenage Daughter
programme.desc: Peter runs up his tab at Mort’s Pharmacy. When he doesn’t have the money to pay, he decides to sell Meg to the Goldmans. Neil is dating another girl which makes Meg jealous, and Stewie falls in love with Liddane, his new babysitter.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg is erroneously reported to have died in a car crash, she goes along with the story to start a new life elsewhere. Meanwhile, the Griffins experience a life without Meg Griffin.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian becomes romantically interested in someone and it inspires him to use Stewie's time machine. Strange consequences result from their journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg discovers her tears produce delicious cookies, and her, along with her siblings, enjoy a profit until the success makes her too happy to cry. Meanwhile, Peter and the guys launch a "male talk show."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian wins a boat on "The Price is Right" and decides to sail to Texas to help women seeking legal abortions.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins attempt to conquer Mount Everest after competitive friends mention they plan to scale the legendary peak. Things, however, are not looking up for the Griffins, as a wicked storm leaves them pinned down on the side of the mountain.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter hires the town's new lawyer, Brick Baker, to represent him in litigation against his new neighbor -- who turns out to be Brick; Brian and Stewie become lobstermen and save Rupert from the perils of the deep.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the family fails to help Lois with the Christmas shopping, she walks out on the family and the Griffins must try to save Christmas on their own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg joins the Adam West High basketball team, and her play is fueled by Peter's lambasting from the crowd. Meanwhile, Stewie goes down a rabbit hole of conspiracies, which ultimately ends with him developing multiple personalities.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter’s sister, Karen, decides to come for Thanksgiving and bullies Peter the same way Meg treats him. While Meg is at first amused, she later feels badly and decides to help Peter train to defeat his sister in a wrestling match. Meanwhile, to avoid overeating on Thanksgiving, Stewie and Brian decide to go on a cleanse, which Stewie takes one step too far.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter makes it through a bad flu season without getting sick, the hospital asks him to volunteer as a nurse, but he finds himself at a crossroads when his mortal enemy, The Giant Chicken, is admitted to the ER. Meanwhile, Brian receives his genetic test results and learns he is part cat, causing him to have an identity crisis.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys try to help Mayor Wild West reconnect with his estranged father, Old West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the owner of The Drunken Clam is killed, Peter and the guys must find a way to save the bar, after the bank threatens to close it. Meanwhile, Meg gets a part-time job at a funeral home.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes the leader of a group of mothers who want to ban books from schools.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's friends roast him. With his feelings hurt, he seeks refuge by befriending a group of gossipy women.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins tell their own versions of three HBO shows: "Game of Thrones," "Succession" and "Big Little Lies."

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois decide to open a cookie store, but Peter ruins things when he decides to bring clients in his own way.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Hard Times at Adam West High
programme.desc: Chris uses Stewie's time machine to stop himself from having an erection during a book report. Peter becomes jealous when he sits out of a lottery pool and the guys win.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In an anthology episode, the cast reenacts three classic pieces of literature commonly taught in high school: "The Great Gatsby," "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" and "Of Mice and Men".

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie ties the knot with his old flame Olivia, but their relationship quickly turns, and becomes normal dull marriage. It all escalates when they go on a double date with Brian and Jillian. Meanwhile, Lois introduces Peter to chick flicks, so he decides to make his own movie with a plot based on every chick movie ever.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie gets a mail order bride from Ukraine and experiences domestic life. Meanwhile, Peter and Chris become addicted to free hotel breakfast buffets.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Stewie's new best friend falls ill, Lois takes him to the hospital where she finds out he has a critical, but treatable, disease. However, the boy's parents refuse treatment due to their religious beliefs, leaving Lois no choice but to take drastic measures to seek treatment for the boy herself. Meanwhile, Peter has a religious experience of his own.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a yacht race that the Griffins win through disobeying Lois' father, Lois decides to fulfill her dream of being a model, which her father never allowed. After she begins partying and using drugs, it is up to Peter and Lois' father to bring her back.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and the guys become shipwrecked at Fire Island; Chris and Brian perform a lobotomy on a social-media-crazed Stewie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter swaps lives with Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh and is forced to make a ruling on the constitutionality of gay marriage, meanwhile, Brett tries to become friends with Quagmire, Cleveland and Joe.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian become overwhelmed after winning a coffee shop. Peter and his friends annoy Meg at her new lifeguard job when they don't follow the pool rules.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire admits that he is in love with Lois. Unfortunately, he proclaims his love while he has accidentally pocket-dialed Peter. Peter is furious and Brian takes it upon himself to fix the situation.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To take advantage of a work policy granting leave in the event of a pet's death, Peter adopts the oldest bird he can find... and realizes it used to belong to Hitler. Meanwhile, after giving Stewie a signed copy of his book, Brian finds it in a free library, causing Brian to give up writing.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To celebrate Peter and Lois' wedding anniversary, the Griffins present their own versions of three well-known love stories.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets carried away when the Nielsen people select the Griffins to have their television-viewing habits monitored and he steals a number of the Nielsen boxes in hopes of controlling the airwaves.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: At his high school reunion, Peter tries to impress his fellow classmates. When Peter, heading for the bathroom, charges through the crowd of people, impresses quarterback Tom Brady, who offers Peter a spot on the New England Patriots. Meanwhile, Brian owes Stewie money, after Stewie becomes a sports bookie.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In search of Stewie's cherished big wheel tricycle, that Peter threw out, Stewie and Brian embark on an adventure to "Garbage Island," a giant floating mass of trash out in the sea. Meanwhile, Lois tries to help Quagmire elevate his dating game, as he courts a middle-aged woman.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter takes his family on a road trip to Bend, Oregon to go to the last Blockbuster so they can have a movie night. But the Griffins road trip leads to disastrous consequences for them after Peter is unable to pay a late fee on a movie he has forgotten to return and the family gets chased by Blockbuster employees.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: A Few More Ways to Die in the West
programme.desc: In this Griffin family spoof of the Old West, Peter, known as Quiet Burp, and his gang of outlaws agrees to escort a wealthy businessman on a train journey.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Ryan Reynolds pays a visit to Quahog and becomes obsessed with Peter Griffin. Meanwhile, Stewie tries to be a big boy and steals the family car for a joy ride but lands in a bit of trouble.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter learns from a psychic through a palm reading that he had a fascinating past life in the 17th century as Griffin Peterson, a dignified English gentleman who vied for the love of Lady Redbush in the newly founded American colony of Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Brian has a near-death experience in the hospital, he decides to lead a new life. He ends up getting a nightly singing gig with Frank Sinatra Jr., but his new lifestyle doesn't last very long. Meanwhile, Meg, after joining a new after-school group, pretends to be a lesbian.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Are You There God? It's Me, Peter
programme.desc: After Peter falls into a coma, Peter meets God face-to-face, which leads him to ask some of life's tough questions.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie tries to civilize his half-brother, Boston Stewie, so that he may be adopted. Meanwhile, Meg and Chris start a venture in which people online pay Chris to sit on and smash cupcakes.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter is mistaken for being a single parent, and relishes in the perks that come with it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg agrees to be Bruce and Jeffery's surrogate causing a pregnant Meg to annoy her family. But, when the couple fails to pick up the baby, the Griffins must take care of their new family.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire meets an avid dog lover, and pretends Brian is his dog in an attempt to win her over. Meanwhile, Chris helps Stewie track down the culprit who stole his Halloween candy.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After going on a date with a Cuban-American woman named Carmen, Brian inexplicably develops a Cuban accent- which he worries will hurt his chances with her.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys recount their first true love stories, leading to parodies of "Castaway," "Dirty Dancing" and various Meg Ryan rom-coms.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Meg suddenly becomes internet famous, she and an excited Peter work together to build her following and create content for her new image that revolves around unhealthy eating. Meanwhile, Brian and Stewie revel in a new ride while Brian's car gets repaired.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Chris is forbidden to see Jerome's daughter Pam, so when the kids run away together, Jerome and Peter must work together to find them.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter realizes Joe has never found him funny, he is determined to make Joe laugh; Lois chaperones Chris and Meg's school trip, where she strives to reunite the principal with his long-lost love.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter becomes friends with a cool, handsome paramedic named Stryker Foxx. Wrapped up in his newfound "coolness," he soon dumps Quagmire, Joe and Cleveland.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg pursues an unlikely friendship with an NFL great; Stewie and Brian host a dinner party at a home furnishing store.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In an effort to improve his writing, Brian convinces Stewie to go back in time and bring Mark Twain to the present day, but the plan goes awry when Twain overindulges in pornography.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: One Foot in Front of the Mother
programme.desc: Due to a police complaint about his excessive barking, Brian has to wear a shock collar; looking to join an after-school team, Chris discovers a talent for speed walking.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Carter Pewterschmidt cancels the annual Quahog Christmas carnival, because he hates the holiday, Peter must transform his father-in-law from a Scrooge into a hometown hero in order to salvage the event. Meanwhile, Stewie devises a master plan to get the one and only thing he wants for Christmas.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg tells Ruth Brian and Stewie have been abducted after she got a phone call from the kidnappers and told her mom Lois. Meg and her friend go to Paris to solve the Mystery before it's too late to save both of them. In Paris Brian and Stewie get kidnapped, raped and held for ransom.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The guys regale each other with stories of youthful athletic accomplishments. Cleveland was a teenage baseball player from Cuba, Quagmire was a bad boy tennis superstar from New York and Peter was an underdog boxer from Philadelphia.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter, Quagmire and Joe visit the Harvard University campus, Quagmire accidentally marries a prostitute. In order to get out of his marriage, Quagmire pretends to be lovers with Peter.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Louis find themselves with a golden opportunity to reconnect as a couple after Lois' parents get a nanny to take care of the children for them.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter, Quagmire, Joe and Cleveland form a neighborhood detective agency, and are stunned to discover that Chris is a thief. Meanwhile, Lois is worried about Chris' delusional relationship with his new girlfriend, but also sees it as an opportunity for Peter to copy Chris' romantic gestures.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Peter's hair turns white from a ghost-hunting adventure in his house with the guys, his new look grabs the attention of local news anchor Tom Tucker. Under Tucker's guidance, he learns the ways of reporting fake news, and soon becomes a senior crime analyst on Quahog's Channel 5 News. Meanwhile, Brian and Stewie attempt to help Chris improve his dating game.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian discovers his nose can detect diseases and smells that someone in the Griffin house has cancer.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and Peter are divided by differing views on who the next Mayor of Quahog should be. Upset at Lois' first choice, Peter seeks out the late Mayor Adam West's cousin, Wild Wild West.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and his friends are sick of forgetting the million-dollar business ideas they come up with while drinking, so they strap on Joe's police body cams to record their every move. Meanwhile, Quagmire is trying to come to terms with an unexpected family development.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After successfully landing a plane, Brian and Quagmire go on a press tour. Meanwhile Chris and Stewie open a boutique pizza place.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins provide DVD commentary on a recent episode and reveal never-before-told drama between Peter and Lois.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Breaking Out Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: Lois starts finding herself compelled to steal things from stores, getting an almost sexual rush from breaking the law. She eventually gets caught, however, and is sent to prison. Unable to cope without her, the family decides to break her out of jail. On the run from the law, they try to make a new life for themselves while hiding out in Asiantown.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter convinces Brian to move in with his girlfriend Jillian and Stewie tags along to help pay the rent but he tries to interfere with their relationship. Meanwhile Meg and Chris get jobs at the convenience store where Chris befriends the manager Carl who leaves Meg to do all the work. Carl soon fires her and promotes Chris to the co-boss.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian's new job as a journalist leads him to uncover that Pawtucket Pat, Quahog's hero and brewery founder, took part in racist actions against Native Americans. The town becomes divided on whether or not to remove the statue that commemorates him.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Wi-Fi goes out in the Griffin house, leaving the family with nothing to do for the evening. In order to keep the family entertained, Peter and Lois decide to tell the kids the story of how they met and fell in love in the '90s.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter encourages Meg to attend a high school party, where she takes up drinking, which both concerns and delights her father. Meanwhile, Brian begins spying on his neighbors and suspects Principal Shepherd of a crime.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois embarks on a journey of self-discovery.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After a mishap at work, Peter decides to take his health more seriously and diet with the help of Quagmire, Joe, and Cleveland. When Lois takes a stab at writing a romantic fantasy novel, a curious Brian and Stewie investigate to find out who she might have a crush.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian lands a dream job at The New Yorker, but gets fired when it's revealed that he dropped out of college. Frustrated, he decides to turn his life around by going back to school and finishing his degree. Peter and his buddies, meanwhile, win an 80's TV look-alike contest dressed as the A-Team, and find that today's world still has a place for a team of renegades who can get the job done.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: On the search to find a new hobby, Chris meets a puppeteer named Franz, an old friend of Herbert's. Despite Herbert's warnings, Chris and Franz remain friends until Franz's checkered past is suddenly revealed.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter hears Loretta scream, he tries to help out, but discovers Loretta is cheating on Cleveland. Peter figures out that it was Quagmire and decides to tell Cleveland about it. But when Loretta walks out on Cleveland he doesn't take it lightly.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois becomes a stand-up comic, using real life stories about Peter as content; Peter tries his best to straighten his life out to avoid being ridiculed by Lois's act.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian get into a friendship-ending fight after Brian commits a terrible act to one of Stewie's beloved toys. Meanwhile, Peter refuses to wash his hand after meeting his favorite breakfast cereal mascot, Boo Berry.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When the Griffins win a trip to Maine at Peter's company picnic, they decide to invite their friends on the vacation. Tensions run high and things soon spiral out of control when the cabin burns down.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Most Interesting Man in the World
programme.desc: When Peter takes Stewie to the park for some quality father-son time, he mistakenly takes the wrong baby home, prompting Lois to call him an idiot. Determined to prove to her that he's a responsible adult, Peter goes on a business trip to Chicago and returns as an intelligent, classy person.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Cleveland returns to town, but is instantly banned from spending time with Peter when Lois and Donna get into a big fight over parenting.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With her vision waning, Lois elects to get LASIK surgery, but exploits her new disability for monetary gain. Meanwhile, Peter, Chris and Stewie must partake in all the activities with Doug that his father neglects.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After attending obedience school, Brian becomes much more submissive, and Peter realizes he misses his old, feistier pal. Meanwhile, Chris makes a new friend at school, but feels betrayed when he discovers it was all a ploy to get closer to Meg.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the help of an out-of-this-world remote control, Stewie and Brian travel through alternate universes, including a post-apocalyptic world and a parallel world run by dogs where humans are pets.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter buys a farm and becomes a meth dealer.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg gets married in Russia but returns home after discovering she was used for a green card marriage. Meanwhile, Chris, in need of an extracurricular activity, joins Principal Shepherd in shooting pornos at the high school.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter makes a hologram of himself in order to get out of menial household duties.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: To help promote the brewery to millennials, Peter adopts the millennial lifestyle, and his methods soon attract the attention of a high-powered Silicon Valley executive.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Pawtucket Brewery hires new bosses, who decide to change the company's mascot. In an attempt to be crowned the new face of Pawtucket Ale, Peter and Brian go head-to-head.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian is smitten with a female show dog and enters a show dog competition in which the winner gets to breed her; Peter and Lois go to extreme measures to try to convince Chris that Arthur Valentine doesn't exist.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Rob Gronkowski moves into the house behind the Griffins. Stewie raises bees and sells honey at the farmers market.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg becomes a getaway driver for Seymour, but it is soon discovered she was hallucinating after she consumed expired mustard packets and Chris is able to cover for Meg for her latest stunt. Meanwhile, Quagmire has hiccups and persuades Peter to kill him if his hiccups continue.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Impelled by nostalgia, Peter recreates movie scenes from his favorite decade; Stewie and Doug compete to retrieve a Frisbee stuck at the top of the jungle gym.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter fills in for Santa at the mall, but lets the power go to his head when he realises just what he can get away with. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian become holiday office-party crashers.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter spends more time with Meg, Lois and the Guys instead of Stewie, Brian and Chris during an apocalypse which was a later found out it was prank. Meg and Lois try to help Peter get the Chris, Stewie and Brian back after he hurt them by calling them boring.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Fonz
programme.desc: When Peter’s dad comes to visit, he tries to impose his religious beliefs on the family. But after a mix-up with the holy-water leaves Stewie in a bubble, Peter decides to form a church worshiping his hero, the Fonz.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: "The Bachelorette" comes to Quahog and Brian is chosen to be a contestant. Brian does not like the show, but participates just to have a vacation only to wind up falling in love with the bachelorette. Meanwhile, Chris gets a nasty pimple on his cheek, which talks to Chris and likes to cause mischief.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When Peter gets hurt and can't work, his boss tells him he needs to speed things up. So Lois goes to the brewery to help him out. Meanwhile, Stewie finds out that Brian is dating a very attractive but not very intelligent woman. He tries to get Brian to break up with her, but Brian can't do it.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets himself arrested to avoid Lois' special Thanksgiving dinner; A secret that Stewie's been keeping from everyone is revealed.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: With the brewery under construction, Lois implores Peter to take a new job, but grows irate when he begins work at the grocery store. Meanwhile, armed with his police stories, Joe and Brian team up to write their own true crime novel.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois and her mom, Babs, have a relaxing spa day, leaving Peter to entertain Lois’ father, Carter. The boys fall for an email scam, and have to go to Africa to get their money back. Meanwhile, Brian pursues Meg’s friend, Patty.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In this three-act parody, the Griffins spoof the stage by putting up To Kill a Mockingbird, The Odd Couple and Hamlet.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: It is Geoffrey's birthday and Will decides to set him up on a blind date as a gift. Will winds up thinking she is perfect and breaks up the two dancing at a nightclub. Later when Geoffrey comes home, he gives Will Helen's phone number stating she would be more fit for Will.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey tries to earn some extra money by entering a medical study, however, there's a slight problem: the study is for identical twins. Rachel and Phoebe take self-defense classes, but Ross attempts to give them a lesson in Unagi instead. Meanwhile, Chandler and Monica agree to give each other homemade presents for Valentine's Day.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
programme.desc: Bart catches a three-eyed fish in the river near Springfield Nuclear Power Plant. When the fish story is featured prominently in the local news, the plant is inspected and then fined millions of dollars for its many safety violations. Infuriated, Mr. Burns decides to run for governor so he can do away with the regulations that are costing him so much money.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: When classified news is shared that the King is to travel through Warmington-on-Sea by train, Capt. Mainwaring and the platoon are detailed to form a guard of honour. Then, when another train breaks down on the main line, blocking the King's route, the platoon swings into action.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: In the midst of the forest flows a stream that splashes and trickles endlessly along – Bob Ross depicts the true beauty of nature.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: When the magic of Equestria mysteriously begins to fail, Twilight leads her friends on a quest for answers, leaving the School of Friendship open to attack from a dangerous mastermind.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: While Twilight and the rest of the Mane Six try to escape Tarturus, Cozy Glow furthers her plot to take over the School of Friendship with only Twilight's students and the CMCs to stop her!

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Will joins the school basketball with Carlton already on it. Will becomes the star of the team and Carlton becomes jealous and the competition between the two starts. Carlton decides to steal the ball from Will and take the game winning shot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer fires up the crowd at a Springfield Isotopes baseball game and is chosen to be the team's new mascot. He immediately becomes a popular attraction and the Isotopes start a winning streak. As a result, Homer is promoted to a team in Capital City, the Capital City Capitals.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon has gone to a camp to undergo an efficiency test. Meanwhile Walker has a van load of onions he's sold to Hodges, who isn't letting them out of his sight, thus he has pursued the platoon to the camp. Later, with no blank shells to use on the test, Mainwaring has a brainwave regarding what to use as ammunition!

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q, and Murr take to the court to serve up some seriously personal tennis lessons, and then head to the park for a bewildering game of Now! And tonight's loser must put his face through the ultimate test during a food presentation!

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard discovers that going into a bank whilst wearing a motorcycle helmet can lead to trouble.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple of equestrian enthusiasts swap suburban life in Farnborough, Hampshire, for a new rural existence in Somerset. Their budget of £600,000 needs to secure not only a country home but also land for their horses. In the country, Nicki wades through mud as she encounters an age old fishing technique.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy decides to get a flock of sheep, not realising the added complications these high-maintenance animals will bring.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc:
The venue for the first programme is La Lanterna in Letchworth, Hertfordshire. Twenty-eight-year-old owner and head chef Alex offers modern Italian cuisine, a taste of Little Italy in England's first garden city, and the restaurant is run by his best mate, maitre d' Gavin, helped by Alex's ex-air hostess girlfriend Emily.
Alex has no customers, cookers that don't work and an expensive menu that's about as authentically Italian as a Hawaiian Pizza. He re-mortgaged his house to buy the business, it's losing £1,000 a week, he hasn't slept for months but still runs round town in a flash car (number plate: A1CHEF) that's worth more than the restaurant. Running out of money, inspiration and energy, Alex is on the verge of losing EVERYTHING.
It's a prime restaurant for a dose of TOUGH LOVE - Ramsay-style. But can a strict diet of brutal honesty, radical food surgery and undiluted energy turn things round, or is it only a matter of time before the lights go out at La Lanterna for good?

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc:
Konata and Yutaka catch a cold, and Yutaka's friends, Minami, Hiyori, and Patricia, come and visit her. They, Konata, and Yutaka discuss younger-sister-moe, Minami's lack of breasts and how Hiyori doesn't want Yutaka to read her doujin manga.
After seeing a TV program about how xylitol chewing gum can eliminate cavities, Miyuki decides to test this out. As a result, one of her fillings comes out and she has to go to the dentist.
Kagami and Misao play a video game together. Although Misao is terrible at playing them, she seems to be having more fun than Kagami who's playing to win.
Patricia tells Kagami what music she's been listening to lately and how much she would like to have a maid.
Miyuki tells her mother about her friends' opinions on Minami, but they seem to contradict with what Miyuki's mom recalls from Minami's previous actions.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: From police chases to the inside scoop on Daisy the Donkey, Channel 6 Elmore News has all the top local stories. Experienced newscaster Kip Schlezinger brings the latest news, sports and weather, even when there is absolutely nothing to report.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry asks unanswerable questions about animals which begin with the letter H, with Ruby Wax, Ross Noble, Sean Lock and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Mackenzie Crook, Chris Packham, Victoria Coren, Rhod Gilbert
programme.desc: In this episode, David is joined by Mackenzie Crook and Chris Packham, while Lee Mack is joined by Victoria Coren and Rhod Gilbert.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House splits his time between treating a firefighter for uncontrollable shivering, giving Tritter a piece of his mind, and handling his trial for forging prescriptions.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Singing: The town of Elmore becomes an all-singing, all-dancing mash up. Everyone has a song inside them, but do they really need to be heard? / The Best: No matter what Gumball does, Carmen thinks she knows better. Gumball attempts to gain the moral high ground but fails spectacularly. He decides that self-improvement isn't his style. The only option he has left is to bring Carmen down to his level.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads hand over testing duties to their mothers to review a range of new small cars:. They test the Renault Modus, Honda Jazz and Peugeot 1007. Jeremy drives the Cadillac CTS-V and didn't like it that much. Richard tests 'Mondeo man's' new favourite car, the new BMW 3 Series. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Omid Djalili.

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: Karl travels to Africa to see wild gorillas. Prior to visiting the gorillas, Ricky and Steve send Karl to teach at a school, rebuild a hut, go bungee jumping, visit a domesticated hippo, cook for a king, and go animal tracking.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: One year has passed since the events of "The Sound of Drums." The Earth has been conquered and its population enslaved, the Doctor is the Master's prisoner, and the warships of a new Time Lord Empire rise from the ashes. The fate of the world is in Martha Jones' hands.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Dates a Student
programme.desc: At the end of semester Ross gets his student evaluations and one of them writes that he's hot. It turns out to be a cute girl named Elizabeth and even though it could cost him his job, Ross considers dating her. An ex-girlfriend of Chandler's from college is directing the new Al Pacino movie and Joey asks Chandler for help with getting an audition. Meanwhile, after a fire ruins Phoebe and Rachel's apartment, they're forced to find temporary living spaces with Monica and Joey.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc:
A special constable gaves a ticket to a motorist. Penny warns a car owner to move his car and tells the special constable to destroy the ticket for the car. Penny loses it after reading something on the toilet mirror.
Dashwood goes to see a man about a burglary. Dashwood is suspious because there are no signs of forced entry. Turns out to be an ex-lover. Burnside won't help because of armed robbers on the manor. Tony and Tosh watch in a pub while the man is approached by his ex-lover.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Norika talks to a driver who parks illegally. She then deals with an upset drug addict at a chemist. The girl is later found badly beaten and her prescription missing. Dashwood is worried that there is something fishy about the prescription. Dashwood checks out her doctor. Tim Able is upset at how a father reacted to being asked about his son's tax disc. Frazer is concerned about Able and Garfield's statement taking. She sets up a role play situation so they can practice. It is interrupted by a gas mains leak.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Will's mom comes to the Banks' for Thanksgiving. She gets angry that the kids of the house leave all of the work to Geoffrey. After Vy criticizes Vivian for being to easy on the kids, she decides to make them cook Thanksgiving dinner. Carlton also invites his English teacher to the dinner.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Joey's fridge breaks, he tries to cajole his friends into helping him buy a new one. Meanwhile, Ralph Lauren is organizing a charity ball and Rachel looks for a date, however, unexpectedly it turns into a competition between Monica & Chandler against Phoebe for finding the best candidate. Ross continues to date Elizabeth, but he gets nervous when she makes plans for a spring break at Daytona Beach.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Todd Flanders decide to enter a miniature golf tournament. Homer becomes confident that Bart will win and makes a bet with Ned that the father of the boy that does not win will have to mow their neighbors lawn in their wife's Sunday dress.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: When a relative of Sgt. Wilson dies, he inherits his title. Then, when a Russian factory worker visits the town to boost Anglo-Russian relations, Mainwaring is asked to present the key to the town - until the mayor hears about Wilson's title, and asks Mainwaring to step down.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross will fascinate you with this crimson winter scene, where cabin and snow are virtually undisturbed and the evergreens are full.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Princess Celestia and Princess Luna decide to retire and hand off the ruling of Equestria to Twilight Sparkle and her friends, which sends Twilight into a tailspin of self-doubt. Meanwhile, an ancient villain bands together a legion of nefarious characters to conquer all of Equestria, challenging Twilight Sparkle and the rest of the Mane 6 to step in and save the day.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: As Twilight and the rest of the Mane six struggle to come to terms with the destruction of the Tree and Elements of Harmony, King Sombra sets his sights on conquering all of Equestria.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Hilary rats Will out for several things including getting in at 3 in the morning the previous day in order to get the family car instead of Will. Will plans revenge, while going to find out where Hilary is shopping he learns that she dropped out of college three months ago. Will uses this in order to get Hilary to slave for him while he is punished. Hilary tries to get Carlton to go against Will, but he also uses it against her.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa makes a table centerpiece for the Thanksgiving dinner, which Bart accidentally destroys. After he is sent to his room by his parents, Bart angrily runs away from home and experiences a very different kind of Thanksgiving holiday.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: Things That Go Bump in the Night
programme.desc:
The platoon gets lost on a wild and stormy night and has to take shelter in an old and deserted house that is used for training tracking dogs. When Pike gets his uniform wet he changes it for an old German uniform, but when the dogs get loose, they chase Pike and the platoon across the fields.
Note: Last regular appearance of James Beck as Pte. Joe Walker. Walker only appears in the external scenes as Beck was taken ill shortly before the studio scenes were recorded.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Q, Murr, Sal, and Joe provide a false sense of security to some sassy shoppers at a drug store, and the competition reaches new heights during a rematch of their balloon challenge in the grocery store. And tonight the guys laugh and cry their way through the first ever permanent punishment!

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons compete over who has the worst life.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin house hunts with a couple who want to escape urban life and find a rural home in Norfolk for their family and horses on a budget of £500,000. While in the county, Jonnie takes control of some seriously heavy machinery at the UK's largest privately owned military collection.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson decides to open a farm shop to sell his own produce. This sounds like it should be easy. It isn't.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc:
Mexican-born Israel and his English partner Tara have sunk a cool £150,000 of their own money into their trendy cafe bar D-Place, which offers a vast selection of fusion cuisine.
They believed they had found the WINNING FORMULA: great place, great music, great service, great food... and all at the right price.
Sounds promising... but from there on it's all DOWNHILL.
For the first two years, it all went swimmingly as Israel built up a mini-empire of five businesses... but the success didn't last long. Just three years later and they are all GONE - only D-Place remains and it's hanging by a thread.
It's a 24-carat NIGHTMARE.
The staff are at WAR, the plastic food is DIRE, the customers have evaporated, the restaurant is losing MONEY and the owners are at their WITS END. Something simply has to give or everyone is out of a job.
Faced with dysfunctional staff and a disastrous menu, Gordon is about to face the LONGEST WEEK of his professional life. Can he quell the panic, banish the deep fa

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc:
It's almost time for the school's culture festival. While the girls are preparing for their classes' themes for the festival, Patricia proposes that she and the girls in her class should do a cheerleading act for the opening ceremony of the festival. Patricia "persuades" Konata into performing the routine with her, who in turn gets Kagami, Tsukasa, Miyuki, Misao, and Ayano to do it as well.
Akira Kogami finally makes an appearance on the actual show, although she and Shiraishi are still on bad terms.
Before the opening ceremony, the girls practice the cheerleading act one last time to the song, "Motteke! Sailor Fuku!"

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole finally gets a raise and an employee of the month plaque, Richard feels that he doesn't get enough credit for his work around the house. As a result, he goes on strike, causing the kids to revert to terrifying monsters.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry asks unanswerable questions about happiness, with Andy Hamilton, Rich Hall, Phill Jupitus and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Lorraine Kelly, Dara Ó Briain, Barry Cryer, Sue Perkins
programme.desc: David is joined by Lorraine Kelly and Dara O Briain, while Lee Mack is joined by Barry Cryer and Sue Perkins.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: While House is forced to work full-time in the clinic and deal with a rape victim who insists on confiding with him, Cameron deals with a terminal cancer patient trying to take advantage of her state of mind.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Leslie's petals begin falling off, he feels like his good looks are fading, so Gumball and Darwin use everything they know about style and beauty to try and help him.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.sub-title: Clarkson Compares the Boxster S and SLK 55 Under Sniper Fire
programme.desc: Former F1 World Champion Damon Hill sets a time in the Liana as a Racing Driver in a Reasonably Priced Car. The Aston Martin DB5 and Jaguar E-Type are compared; the snipers of the Irish Guards try to shoot Jeremy as he drives the new Mercedes SLK and the new Porsche Boxster 2 around a deserted village. James drives the new 4x4 from Nissan, the Murano and Richard travels to Italy to drive the Maserati Gran Sport around the Maserati test track.

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: Karl wants to drive along the legendary Route 66 in the USA in an open top Cadillac but Ricky and Steve have other ideas. Along the way they want him join a high school choir, hang out with New Age therapists, meet the Amish before ending his journey by Wingwalking.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: Trainee doctor Martha Jones has her hands full balancing her medical studies with placating her demanding family. So the last thing she needs is to find that the hospital where she works has been transported to the moon, where it's invaded by an overbearing alien police force. There she encounters a patient called John Smith, who seems curiously at ease with the situation…

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E.
programme.desc: Joey gets an audition for the lead character in a new TV show. When the audition is rescheduled, Chandler takes the message but forgets to tell Joey. When Joey misses the audition, he gets upset and the group reminisces about their mishaps in the past.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Conway talks to the relief about image. ""Efficient service with a smile"". Martella is asked to do surgery that afternoon. Booze has been found in the locker room. Conway is on the war path. Edwards goes for counseling. Taffy thinks it will be a one-off but the counselor thinks different. The counselor thinks he made headway until Taffy makes a comment about wasting time. Taffy sends his wife to Wales to look for a house for them. Able on a bike catches a lady getting out of a car used in a wages snatch. Stamp is not happy he has to transport the dog handler around.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A amn at a caryard has complaints about kids damaging cars and nicking car radios. The kids harass a man who has moved back into his old family home. The man snapped after the kids came back and grabbed one of them. Stamp and Melvin evict a retirement party from a pub. The workers return to the factory and continue the party there.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Ashley is having a sleepover, and the girls ask Will to tells them a scary story. He recounts the horrifying tale of the first day he started going to Bel-Air Academy - a place that is revealed to be an all boys school (meaning no fly honeys). More terrors ensue when he is introduced to the glee club, square teachers. His classmates seem to like him but he causes a problem when he carves his name in the alumni school desk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Meets Elizabeth's Dad
programme.desc: Ross meets Elizabeth's father for the first time and unfortunately he takes an instant disliking to Ross. However Paul does hit it off with Rachel and she tries to talk Ross up but does so with mixed results. Joey almost gets fired from "Mac and C.H.E.E.S.E." after offending the operator of the robot. Meanwhile, Phoebe writes a book based on Monica and Chandler.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons go to a Monster truck rally that features famous daredevil Lance Murdock. Bart immediately becomes enamored and decides that he wants to become a daredevil as well. His first stunt ends in injury, but to gain the respect of his friends, he promises a far more impressive feat to them: to jump the Springfield Gorge.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc:
Capt. Mainwaring has gone into hospital for an operation on ingrowing toenails. Meanwhile Wilson as acting CO has asked the vicar and the verger to join the platoon to make up the numbers.
Note: This is the first episode not to feature James Beck as Pte. Joe Walker, although Beck is still included in the closing credits.
